{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = N29:N35-N29:N35-X1 |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Qenqenet
(Qen qenet)
''Qn qn.t'' }} '''Qenqenet''' refers to the celestial region just above the {{ill|Horizon|de|Horizont}} in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie}} and {{ill|Astronomy in Ancient Egypt|de|Astronomie im Alten Ägypten}}. Specifically, it contrasts with the {{ill|Mesqet|de|Mesqet}} and denotes the entrance area to the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat}}. == Background == [[File:Mock mirage of the setting sun.jpg|mini|Qenqenet zone: Sunset over the horizon]] The Qenqenet is the place where {{ill|Ra (deity)|de|Re (ägyptische Mythologie)}}, {{ill|Horus|de|Horus}}, and the {{ill|Decan stars|de|Dekan-Stern}} reside at the beginning of {{ill|Sunset|de|Sonnenuntergang}}, just before they descend fully below the horizon. Mythologically, Qenqenet is therefore known as "the place before {{ill|Reincarnation|de|Wiedergeburt}}." The location of Qenqenet was long debated among {{ill|Egyptologists|de|Ägyptologe}}, as many texts also refer to it as "a region in the sky and the Duat." Qenqenet plays an important role in the {{ill|Pyramid Texts|de|Pyramidentexte}}. Scholars such as {{ill|Kurt Sethe|de|Kurt Sethe}},Kurt Sethe: ''Die altägyptischen Pyramidentexte. Nach den Papierabdrücken und Photographien des Berliner Museums. 1, Text, 1. Hälfte, Spruch 1-468 (Pyr. 1-905).'' Olms, Hildesheim 2001 (Nachdruck von 1908), ISBN 3-487-02593-0, S. 315. {{ill|Arielle Kozloff|de|Arielle Kozloff}},Arielle Kozloff et al.: ''Egypt’s dazzling sun: Amenhotep III and his world.'' Cleveland Museum of Art in cooperation with Indiana University Press, Cleveland 1992, ISBN 0-940717-16-6, S. 336–337. and {{ill|Ronald Wells|de|Ronald Wells}}Ronald Wells: ''The Mythology of Nut and the Birth of Ra.'' In: ''Studien zur altägyptischen Kultur.'' Band 19, 1992, S. 305–321. proposed that it could be the {{ill|Milky Way|de|Milchstraße}}. However, further studies of other texts dismissed this hypothesis, as other evidence clearly referred to it as a "transitional region of the {{ill|Sky|de|Himmel (planetär)}} and the {{ill|Stars|de|Stern}}." Extensive studies by {{ill|Harco Willems|de|Harco Willems}},Harco Willems: ''The coffin of Heqata (Cairo JdE 36418): A case study of Egyptian funerary culture of the Early Middle Kingdom.'' Peeters, Leuven 1996, ISBN 90-6831-769-5, S. 262–270. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss}},Rolf Krauss: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5, S. 254–255. and {{ill|Arno Egberts|de|Arno Egberts}}Arno Egberts: ''In quest of meaning: A study of the ancient Egyptian rites of consecrating the meret chests and driving the calves.'' Nederlands Institut voor het Nabije Osten, Leiden 1995, ISBN 90-6258-208-7, S. 292–293. confined Qenqenet to the horizon's vicinity. Analysis of {{ill|Papyrus|de|Papyrus}} {{ill|Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|de|pBM 47.218.50}} concerning Mesqet sanctuariesJean-Claude Goyon: ''Confirmation du pouvoir royal au nouvel an. [Brooklyn Museum 47.218.50]. Planches'' (= ''Bibliothèque d'Étude.'' [BdE] Band 52). Institut Francais d'Archéologie Orientale (IFAO), Kairo 1972, S. 93–94. aligned with newer interpretations that identified Qenqenet explicitly with the eastern and western horizons. The {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch}} clarified its assignment to the gateway regions of the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat}}, just above the horizon. In the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Book of the Dead|de|Ägyptisches Totenbuch}}, Qenqenet is described in detail: "When Ra sets in Qenqenet, he can do anything there, just as one acts on the {{ill|Island of Flames|de|Flammeninsel}}," which directly relates to the winding Duat watercourse {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha}} and the fields of {{ill|Aaru|de|Sechet-iaru|kk=Aaru|lt=Sekhet-iaru}}." {{Quote|{{ill|Horus|de|Horus}}, the {{ill|Falcon|de|Falken}} 1,000 {{ill|Meh|de|Meh}} long, rises and sets in Qenqenet, his birthplace and waterways. O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in {{ill|Sekhet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru}}), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|de|Bertha Porter}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind Moss}}: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[de:Qenqenet]]

O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in {{ill|Sekhet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru}}), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|de|Bertha Porter}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind Moss}}: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[de:Qenqenet]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = N29:N35-N29:N35-X1 |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Qenqenet
(Qen qenet)
''Qn qn.t'' }} '''Qenqenet''' refers to the celestial region just above the {{ill|Horizon|de|Horizont}} in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie}} and {{ill|Astronomy in Ancient Egypt|de|Astronomie im Alten Ägypten}}. Specifically, it contrasts with the {{ill|Mesqet|de|Mesqet}} and denotes the entrance area to the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat}}. == Background == [[File:Mock mirage of the setting sun.jpg|mini|Qenqenet zone: Sunset over the horizon]] The Qenqenet is the place where {{ill|Ra (deity)|de|Re (ägyptische Mythologie)}}, {{ill|Horus|de|Horus}}, and the {{ill|Decan stars|de|Dekan-Stern}} reside at the beginning of {{ill|Sunset|de|Sonnenuntergang}}, just before they descend fully below the horizon. Mythologically, Qenqenet is therefore known as "the place before {{ill|Reincarnation|de|Wiedergeburt}}." The location of Qenqenet was long debated among {{ill|Egyptologists|de|Ägyptologe}}, as many texts also refer to it as "a region in the sky and the Duat." Qenqenet plays an important role in the {{ill|Pyramid Texts|de|Pyramidentexte}}. Scholars such as {{ill|Kurt Sethe|de|Kurt Sethe}},Kurt Sethe: ''Die altägyptischen Pyramidentexte. Nach den Papierabdrücken und Photographien des Berliner Museums. 1, Text, 1. Hälfte, Spruch 1-468 (Pyr. 1-905).'' Olms, Hildesheim 2001 (Nachdruck von 1908), ISBN 3-487-02593-0, S. 315. {{ill|Arielle Kozloff|de|Arielle Kozloff}},Arielle Kozloff et al.: ''Egypt’s dazzling sun: Amenhotep III and his world.'' Cleveland Museum of Art in cooperation with Indiana University Press, Cleveland 1992, ISBN 0-940717-16-6, S. 336–337. and {{ill|Ronald Wells|de|Ronald Wells}}Ronald Wells: ''The Mythology of Nut and the Birth of Ra.'' In: ''Studien zur altägyptischen Kultur.'' Band 19, 1992, S. 305–321. proposed that it could be the {{ill|Milky Way|de|Milchstraße}}. However, further studies of other texts dismissed this hypothesis, as other evidence clearly referred to it as a "transitional region of the {{ill|Sky|de|Himmel (planetär)}} and the {{ill|Stars|de|Stern}}." Extensive studies by {{ill|Harco Willems|de|Harco Willems}},Harco Willems: ''The coffin of Heqata (Cairo JdE 36418): A case study of Egyptian funerary culture of the Early Middle Kingdom.'' Peeters, Leuven 1996, ISBN 90-6831-769-5, S. 262–270. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss}},Rolf Krauss: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5, S. 254–255. and {{ill|Arno Egberts|de|Arno Egberts}}Arno Egberts: ''In quest of meaning: A study of the ancient Egyptian rites of consecrating the meret chests and driving the calves.'' Nederlands Institut voor het Nabije Osten, Leiden 1995, ISBN 90-6258-208-7, S. 292–293. confined Qenqenet to the horizon's vicinity. Analysis of {{ill|Papyrus|de|Papyrus}} {{ill|Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|de|pBM 47.218.50}} concerning Mesqet sanctuariesJean-Claude Goyon: ''Confirmation du pouvoir royal au nouvel an. [Brooklyn Museum 47.218.50]. Planches'' (= ''Bibliothèque d'Étude.'' [BdE] Band 52). Institut Francais d'Archéologie Orientale (IFAO), Kairo 1972, S. 93–94. aligned with newer interpretations that identified Qenqenet explicitly with the eastern and western horizons. The {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch}} clarified its assignment to the gateway regions of the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat}}, just above the horizon. In the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Book of the Dead|de|Ägyptisches Totenbuch}}, Qenqenet is described in detail: "When Ra sets in Qenqenet, he can do anything there, just as one acts on the {{ill|Island of Flames|de|Flammeninsel}}," which directly relates to the winding Duat watercourse {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha}} and the fields of {{ill|Aaru|de|Sechet-iaru|kk=Aaru|lt=Sekhet-iaru}}." {{Quote|{{ill|Horus|de|Horus}}, the {{ill|Falcon|de|Falken}} 1,000 {{ill|Meh|de|Meh}} long, rises and sets in Qenqenet, his birthplace and waterways. O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in {{ill|Sekhet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru}}), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|de|Bertha Porter}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind Moss}}: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[de:Qenqenet]]

Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|de|Bertha Porter}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind Moss}}: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Qenqenet]][[de:Qenqenet]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = N29:N35-N29:N35-X1 |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Qenqenet
(Qen qenet)
''Qn qn.t'' }} '''Qenqenet''' refers to the celestial region just above the {{ill|Horizon|de|Horizont}} in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie}} and {{ill|Astronomy in Ancient Egypt|de|Astronomie im Alten Ägypten}}. Specifically, it contrasts with the {{ill|Mesqet|de|Mesqet}} and denotes the entrance area to the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat}}. == Background == [[File:Mock mirage of the setting sun.jpg|mini|Qenqenet zone: Sunset over the horizon]] The Qenqenet is the place where {{ill|Ra (deity)|de|Re (ägyptische Mythologie)}}, {{ill|Horus|de|Horus}}, and the {{ill|Decan stars|de|Dekan-Stern}} reside at the beginning of {{ill|Sunset|de|Sonnenuntergang}}, just before they descend fully below the horizon. Mythologically, Qenqenet is therefore known as "the place before {{ill|Reincarnation|de|Wiedergeburt}}." The location of Qenqenet was long debated among {{ill|Egyptologists|de|Ägyptologe}}, as many texts also refer to it as "a region in the sky and the Duat." Qenqenet plays an important role in the {{ill|Pyramid Texts|de|Pyramidentexte}}. Scholars such as {{ill|Kurt Sethe|de|Kurt Sethe}},Kurt Sethe: ''Die altägyptischen Pyramidentexte. Nach den Papierabdrücken und Photographien des Berliner Museums. 1, Text, 1. Hälfte, Spruch 1-468 (Pyr. 1-905).'' Olms, Hildesheim 2001 (Nachdruck von 1908), ISBN 3-487-02593-0, S. 315. {{ill|Arielle Kozloff|de|Arielle Kozloff}},Arielle Kozloff et al.: ''Egypt’s dazzling sun: Amenhotep III and his world.'' Cleveland Museum of Art in cooperation with Indiana University Press, Cleveland 1992, ISBN 0-940717-16-6, S. 336–337. and {{ill|Ronald Wells|de|Ronald Wells}}Ronald Wells: ''The Mythology of Nut and the Birth of Ra.'' In: ''Studien zur altägyptischen Kultur.'' Band 19, 1992, S. 305–321. proposed that it could be the {{ill|Milky Way|de|Milchstraße}}. However, further studies of other texts dismissed this hypothesis, as other evidence clearly referred to it as a "transitional region of the {{ill|Sky|de|Himmel (planetär)}} and the {{ill|Stars|de|Stern}}." Extensive studies by {{ill|Harco Willems|de|Harco Willems}},Harco Willems: ''The coffin of Heqata (Cairo JdE 36418): A case study of Egyptian funerary culture of the Early Middle Kingdom.'' Peeters, Leuven 1996, ISBN 90-6831-769-5, S. 262–270. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss}},Rolf Krauss: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5, S. 254–255. and {{ill|Arno Egberts|de|Arno Egberts}}Arno Egberts: ''In quest of meaning: A study of the ancient Egyptian rites of consecrating the meret chests and driving the calves.'' Nederlands Institut voor het Nabije Osten, Leiden 1995, ISBN 90-6258-208-7, S. 292–293. confined Qenqenet to the horizon's vicinity. Analysis of {{ill|Papyrus|de|Papyrus}} {{ill|Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|de|pBM 47.218.50}} concerning Mesqet sanctuariesJean-Claude Goyon: ''Confirmation du pouvoir royal au nouvel an. [Brooklyn Museum 47.218.50]. Planches'' (= ''Bibliothèque d'Étude.'' [BdE] Band 52). Institut Francais d'Archéologie Orientale (IFAO), Kairo 1972, S. 93–94. aligned with newer interpretations that identified Qenqenet explicitly with the eastern and western horizons. The {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch}} clarified its assignment to the gateway regions of the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat}}, just above the horizon. In the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Book of the Dead|de|Ägyptisches Totenbuch}}, Qenqenet is described in detail: "When Ra sets in Qenqenet, he can do anything there, just as one acts on the {{ill|Island of Flames|de|Flammeninsel}}," which directly relates to the winding Duat watercourse {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha}} and the fields of {{ill|Aaru|de|Sechet-iaru|kk=Aaru|lt=Sekhet-iaru}}." {{Quote|{{ill|Horus|de|Horus}}, the {{ill|Falcon|de|Falken}} 1,000 {{ill|Meh|de|Meh}} long, rises and sets in Qenqenet, his birthplace and waterways. O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in {{ill|Sekhet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru}}), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|de|Bertha Porter}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind Moss}}: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Qenqenet]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace ]][[Category:qq]] [[de:Qenqenet]]

Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Qenqenet-qq]] [[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] [[Category:qq-Qenqenet-qq]] [[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Qenqenet]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace ]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]] [[de:Qenqenet]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = N29:N35-N29:N35-X1 |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Qenqenet
(Qen qenet)
''Qn qn.t'' }} '''Qenqenet''' refers to the celestial region just above the {{ill|Horizon|de|Horizont}} in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie}} and {{ill|Astronomy in Ancient Egypt|de|Astronomie im Alten Ägypten}}. Specifically, it contrasts with the {{ill|Mesqet|de|Mesqet}} and denotes the entrance area to the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat}}. == Background == [[File:Mock mirage of the setting sun.jpg|mini|Qenqenet zone: Sunset over the horizon]] The Qenqenet is the place where {{ill|Ra (deity)|de|Re (ägyptische Mythologie)}}, {{ill|Horus|de|Horus}}, and the {{ill|Decan stars|de|Dekan-Stern}} reside at the beginning of {{ill|Sunset|de|Sonnenuntergang}}, just before they descend fully below the horizon. Mythologically, Qenqenet is therefore known as "the place before {{ill|Reincarnation|de|Wiedergeburt}}." The location of Qenqenet was long debated among {{ill|Egyptologists|de|Ägyptologe}}, as many texts also refer to it as "a region in the sky and the Duat." Qenqenet plays an important role in the {{ill|Pyramid Texts|de|Pyramidentexte}}. Scholars such as {{ill|Kurt Sethe|de|Kurt Sethe}},Kurt Sethe: ''Die altägyptischen Pyramidentexte. Nach den Papierabdrücken und Photographien des Berliner Museums. 1, Text, 1. Hälfte, Spruch 1-468 (Pyr. 1-905).'' Olms, Hildesheim 2001 (Nachdruck von 1908), ISBN 3-487-02593-0, S. 315. {{ill|Arielle Kozloff|de|Arielle Kozloff}},Arielle Kozloff et al.: ''Egypt’s dazzling sun: Amenhotep III and his world.'' Cleveland Museum of Art in cooperation with Indiana University Press, Cleveland 1992, ISBN 0-940717-16-6, S. 336–337. and {{ill|Ronald Wells|de|Ronald Wells}}Ronald Wells: ''The Mythology of Nut and the Birth of Ra.'' In: ''Studien zur altägyptischen Kultur.'' Band 19, 1992, S. 305–321. proposed that it could be the {{ill|Milky Way|de|Milchstraße}}. However, further studies of other texts dismissed this hypothesis, as other evidence clearly referred to it as a "transitional region of the {{ill|Sky|de|Himmel (planetär)}} and the {{ill|Stars|de|Stern}}." Extensive studies by {{ill|Harco Willems|de|Harco Willems}},Harco Willems: ''The coffin of Heqata (Cairo JdE 36418): A case study of Egyptian funerary culture of the Early Middle Kingdom.'' Peeters, Leuven 1996, ISBN 90-6831-769-5, S. 262–270. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss}},Rolf Krauss: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5, S. 254–255. and {{ill|Arno Egberts|de|Arno Egberts}}Arno Egberts: ''In quest of meaning: A study of the ancient Egyptian rites of consecrating the meret chests and driving the calves.'' Nederlands Institut voor het Nabije Osten, Leiden 1995, ISBN 90-6258-208-7, S. 292–293. confined Qenqenet to the horizon's vicinity. Analysis of {{ill|Papyrus|de|Papyrus}} {{ill|Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|de|pBM 47.218.50}} concerning Mesqet sanctuariesJean-Claude Goyon: ''Confirmation du pouvoir royal au nouvel an. [Brooklyn Museum 47.218.50]. Planches'' (= ''Bibliothèque d'Étude.'' [BdE] Band 52). Institut Francais d'Archéologie Orientale (IFAO), Kairo 1972, S. 93–94. aligned with newer interpretations that identified Qenqenet explicitly with the eastern and western horizons. The {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch}} clarified its assignment to the gateway regions of the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat}}, just above the horizon. In the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Book of the Dead|de|Ägyptisches Totenbuch}}, Qenqenet is described in detail: "When Ra sets in Qenqenet, he can do anything there, just as one acts on the {{ill|Island of Flames|de|Flammeninsel}}," which directly relates to the winding Duat watercourse {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha}} and the fields of {{ill|Aaru|de|Sechet-iaru|kk=Aaru|lt=Sekhet-iaru}}." {{Quote|{{ill|Horus|de|Horus}}, the {{ill|Falcon|de|Falken}} 1,000 {{ill|Meh|de|Meh}} long, rises and sets in Qenqenet, his birthplace and waterways. O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in {{ill|Sekhet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru}}), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|de|Bertha Porter}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind Moss}}: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Qenqenet-qq]] [[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Qenqenet-qq]] [[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Qenqenet]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace ]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[de:Qenqenet]]

The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Qenqenet-qq]] [[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Qenqenet-qq]] [[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Qenqenet]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace ]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[de:Qenqenet]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = N29:N35-N29:N35-X1 |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Qenqenet
(Qen qenet)
''Qn qn.t'' }} '''Qenqenet''' refers to the celestial region just above the {{ill|Horizon|de|Horizont}} in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie}} and {{ill|Astronomy in Ancient Egypt|de|Astronomie im Alten Ägypten}}. Specifically, it contrasts with the {{ill|Mesqet|de|Mesqet}} and denotes the entrance area to the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat}}. == Background == [[File:Mock mirage of the setting sun.jpg|mini|Qenqenet zone: Sunset over the horizon]] The Qenqenet is the place where {{ill|Ra (deity)|de|Re (ägyptische Mythologie)}}, {{ill|Horus|de|Horus}}, and the {{ill|Decan stars|de|Dekan-Stern}} reside at the beginning of {{ill|Sunset|de|Sonnenuntergang}}, just before they descend fully below the horizon. Mythologically, Qenqenet is therefore known as "the place before {{ill|Reincarnation|de|Wiedergeburt}}." The location of Qenqenet was long debated among {{ill|Egyptologists|de|Ägyptologe}}, as many texts also refer to it as "a region in the sky and the Duat." Qenqenet plays an important role in the {{ill|Pyramid Texts|de|Pyramidentexte}}. Scholars such as {{ill|Kurt Sethe|de|Kurt Sethe}},Kurt Sethe: ''Die altägyptischen Pyramidentexte. Nach den Papierabdrücken und Photographien des Berliner Museums. 1, Text, 1. Hälfte, Spruch 1-468 (Pyr. 1-905).'' Olms, Hildesheim 2001 (Nachdruck von 1908), ISBN 3-487-02593-0, S. 315. {{ill|Arielle Kozloff|de|Arielle Kozloff}},Arielle Kozloff et al.: ''Egypt’s dazzling sun: Amenhotep III and his world.'' Cleveland Museum of Art in cooperation with Indiana University Press, Cleveland 1992, ISBN 0-940717-16-6, S. 336–337. and {{ill|Ronald Wells|de|Ronald Wells}}Ronald Wells: ''The Mythology of Nut and the Birth of Ra.'' In: ''Studien zur altägyptischen Kultur.'' Band 19, 1992, S. 305–321. proposed that it could be the {{ill|Milky Way|de|Milchstraße}}. However, further studies of other texts dismissed this hypothesis, as other evidence clearly referred to it as a "transitional region of the {{ill|Sky|de|Himmel (planetär)}} and the {{ill|Stars|de|Stern}}." Extensive studies by {{ill|Harco Willems|de|Harco Willems}},Harco Willems: ''The coffin of Heqata (Cairo JdE 36418): A case study of Egyptian funerary culture of the Early Middle Kingdom.'' Peeters, Leuven 1996, ISBN 90-6831-769-5, S. 262–270. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss}},Rolf Krauss: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5, S. 254–255. and {{ill|Arno Egberts|de|Arno Egberts}}Arno Egberts: ''In quest of meaning: A study of the ancient Egyptian rites of consecrating the meret chests and driving the calves.'' Nederlands Institut voor het Nabije Osten, Leiden 1995, ISBN 90-6258-208-7, S. 292–293. confined Qenqenet to the horizon's vicinity. Analysis of {{ill|Papyrus|de|Papyrus}} {{ill|Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|de|pBM 47.218.50}} concerning Mesqet sanctuariesJean-Claude Goyon: ''Confirmation du pouvoir royal au nouvel an. [Brooklyn Museum 47.218.50]. Planches'' (= ''Bibliothèque d'Étude.'' [BdE] Band 52). Institut Francais d'Archéologie Orientale (IFAO), Kairo 1972, S. 93–94. aligned with newer interpretations that identified Qenqenet explicitly with the eastern and western horizons. The {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch}} clarified its assignment to the gateway regions of the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat}}, just above the horizon. In the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Book of the Dead|de|Ägyptisches Totenbuch}}, Qenqenet is described in detail: "When Ra sets in Qenqenet, he can do anything there, just as one acts on the {{ill|Island of Flames|de|Flammeninsel}}," which directly relates to the winding Duat watercourse {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha}} and the fields of {{ill|Aaru|de|Sechet-iaru|kk=Aaru|lt=Sekhet-iaru}}." {{Quote|{{ill|Horus|de|Horus}}, the {{ill|Falcon|de|Falken}} 1,000 {{ill|Meh|de|Meh}} long, rises and sets in Qenqenet, his birthplace and waterways. O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in {{ill|Sekhet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru}}), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|de|Bertha Porter}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind Moss}}: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Qenqenet-qq]] [[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Qenqenet-qq]] [[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Qenqenet]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace ]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[de:Qenqenet]]

In the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Book of the Dead|de|Ägyptisches Totenbuch}}, Qenqenet is described in detail: "When Ra sets in Qenqenet, he can do anything there, just as one acts on the {{ill|Island of Flames|de|Flammeninsel}}," which directly relates to the winding Duat watercourse {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha}} and the fields of {{ill|Aaru|de|Sechet-iaru|kk=Aaru|lt=Sekhet-iaru}}." {{Quote|{{ill|Horus|de|Horus}}, the {{ill|Falcon|de|Falken}} 1,000 {{ill|Meh|de|Meh}} long, rises and sets in Qenqenet, his birthplace and waterways. O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in {{ill|Sekhet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru}}), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|de|Bertha Porter}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind Moss}}: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Qenqenet-qq]] [[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Qenqenet-qq]] [[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Qenqenet]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace ]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]] [[de:Qenqenet]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = N29:N35-N29:N35-X1 |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Qenqenet
(Qen qenet)
''Qn qn.t'' }} '''Qenqenet''' refers to the celestial region just above the {{ill|Horizon|de|Horizont}} in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie}} and {{ill|Astronomy in Ancient Egypt|de|Astronomie im Alten Ägypten}}. Specifically, it contrasts with the {{ill|Mesqet|de|Mesqet}} and denotes the entrance area to the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat}}. == Background == [[File:Mock mirage of the setting sun.jpg|mini|Qenqenet zone: Sunset over the horizon]] The Qenqenet is the place where {{ill|Ra (deity)|de|Re (ägyptische Mythologie)}}, {{ill|Horus|de|Horus}}, and the {{ill|Decan stars|de|Dekan-Stern}} reside at the beginning of {{ill|Sunset|de|Sonnenuntergang}}, just before they descend fully below the horizon. Mythologically, Qenqenet is therefore known as "the place before {{ill|Reincarnation|de|Wiedergeburt}}." The location of Qenqenet was long debated among {{ill|Egyptologists|de|Ägyptologe}}, as many texts also refer to it as "a region in the sky and the Duat." Qenqenet plays an important role in the {{ill|Pyramid Texts|de|Pyramidentexte}}. Scholars such as {{ill|Kurt Sethe|de|Kurt Sethe}},Kurt Sethe: ''Die altägyptischen Pyramidentexte. Nach den Papierabdrücken und Photographien des Berliner Museums. 1, Text, 1. Hälfte, Spruch 1-468 (Pyr. 1-905).'' Olms, Hildesheim 2001 (Nachdruck von 1908), ISBN 3-487-02593-0, S. 315. {{ill|Arielle Kozloff|de|Arielle Kozloff}},Arielle Kozloff et al.: ''Egypt’s dazzling sun: Amenhotep III and his world.'' Cleveland Museum of Art in cooperation with Indiana University Press, Cleveland 1992, ISBN 0-940717-16-6, S. 336–337. and {{ill|Ronald Wells|de|Ronald Wells}}Ronald Wells: ''The Mythology of Nut and the Birth of Ra.'' In: ''Studien zur altägyptischen Kultur.'' Band 19, 1992, S. 305–321. proposed that it could be the {{ill|Milky Way|de|Milchstraße}}. However, further studies of other texts dismissed this hypothesis, as other evidence clearly referred to it as a "transitional region of the {{ill|Sky|de|Himmel (planetär)}} and the {{ill|Stars|de|Stern}}." Extensive studies by {{ill|Harco Willems|de|Harco Willems}},Harco Willems: ''The coffin of Heqata (Cairo JdE 36418): A case study of Egyptian funerary culture of the Early Middle Kingdom.'' Peeters, Leuven 1996, ISBN 90-6831-769-5, S. 262–270. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss}},Rolf Krauss: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5, S. 254–255. and {{ill|Arno Egberts|de|Arno Egberts}}Arno Egberts: ''In quest of meaning: A study of the ancient Egyptian rites of consecrating the meret chests and driving the calves.'' Nederlands Institut voor het Nabije Osten, Leiden 1995, ISBN 90-6258-208-7, S. 292–293. confined Qenqenet to the horizon's vicinity. Analysis of {{ill|Papyrus|de|Papyrus}} {{ill|Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|de|pBM 47.218.50}} concerning Mesqet sanctuariesJean-Claude Goyon: ''Confirmation du pouvoir royal au nouvel an. [Brooklyn Museum 47.218.50]. Planches'' (= ''Bibliothèque d'Étude.'' [BdE] Band 52). Institut Francais d'Archéologie Orientale (IFAO), Kairo 1972, S. 93–94. aligned with newer interpretations that identified Qenqenet explicitly with the eastern and western horizons. The {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch}} clarified its assignment to the gateway regions of the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat}}, just above the horizon. In the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Book of the Dead|de|Ägyptisches Totenbuch}}, Qenqenet is described in detail: "When Ra sets in Qenqenet, he can do anything there, just as one acts on the {{ill|Island of Flames|de|Flammeninsel}}," which directly relates to the winding Duat watercourse {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha}} and the fields of {{ill|Aaru|de|Sechet-iaru|kk=Aaru|lt=Sekhet-iaru}}." {{Quote|{{ill|Horus|de|Horus}}, the {{ill|Falcon|de|Falken}} 1,000 {{ill|Meh|de|Meh}} long, rises and sets in Qenqenet, his birthplace and waterways. O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in {{ill|Sekhet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru}}), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|de|Bertha Porter}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind Moss}}: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Qenqenet-qq]] [[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Qenqenet-qq]] [[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Qenqenet]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace ]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]] [[de:Qenqenet]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = N29:N35-N29:N35-X1 |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Qenqenet
(Qen qenet)
''Qn qn.t'' }} '''Qenqenet''' refers to the celestial region just above the {{ill|Horizon|de|Horizont}} in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie}} and {{ill|Astronomy in Ancient Egypt|de|Astronomie im Alten Ägypten}}. Specifically, it contrasts with the {{ill|Mesqet|de|Mesqet}} and denotes the entrance area to the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat}}. == Background == [[File:Mock mirage of the setting sun.jpg|mini|Qenqenet zone: Sunset over the horizon]] The Qenqenet is the place where {{ill|Ra (deity)|de|Re (ägyptische Mythologie)}}, {{ill|Horus|de|Horus}}, and the {{ill|Decan stars|de|Dekan-Stern}} reside at the beginning of {{ill|Sunset|de|Sonnenuntergang}}, just before they descend fully below the horizon. Mythologically, Qenqenet is therefore known as "the place before {{ill|Reincarnation|de|Wiedergeburt}}." The location of Qenqenet was long debated among {{ill|Egyptologists|de|Ägyptologe}}, as many texts also refer to it as "a region in the sky and the Duat." Qenqenet plays an important role in the {{ill|Pyramid Texts|de|Pyramidentexte}}. Scholars such as {{ill|Kurt Sethe|de|Kurt Sethe}},Kurt Sethe: ''Die altägyptischen Pyramidentexte. Nach den Papierabdrücken und Photographien des Berliner Museums. 1, Text, 1. Hälfte, Spruch 1-468 (Pyr. 1-905).'' Olms, Hildesheim 2001 (Nachdruck von 1908), ISBN 3-487-02593-0, S. 315. {{ill|Arielle Kozloff|de|Arielle Kozloff}},Arielle Kozloff et al.: ''Egypt’s dazzling sun: Amenhotep III and his world.'' Cleveland Museum of Art in cooperation with Indiana University Press, Cleveland 1992, ISBN 0-940717-16-6, S. 336–337. and {{ill|Ronald Wells|de|Ronald Wells}}Ronald Wells: ''The Mythology of Nut and the Birth of Ra.'' In: ''Studien zur altägyptischen Kultur.'' Band 19, 1992, S. 305–321. proposed that it could be the {{ill|Milky Way|de|Milchstraße}}. However, further studies of other texts dismissed this hypothesis, as other evidence clearly referred to it as a "transitional region of the {{ill|Sky|de|Himmel (planetär)}} and the {{ill|Stars|de|Stern}}." Extensive studies by {{ill|Harco Willems|de|Harco Willems}},Harco Willems: ''The coffin of Heqata (Cairo JdE 36418): A case study of Egyptian funerary culture of the Early Middle Kingdom.'' Peeters, Leuven 1996, ISBN 90-6831-769-5, S. 262–270. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss}},Rolf Krauss: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5, S. 254–255. and {{ill|Arno Egberts|de|Arno Egberts}}Arno Egberts: ''In quest of meaning: A study of the ancient Egyptian rites of consecrating the meret chests and driving the calves.'' Nederlands Institut voor het Nabije Osten, Leiden 1995, ISBN 90-6258-208-7, S. 292–293. confined Qenqenet to the horizon's vicinity. Analysis of {{ill|Papyrus|de|Papyrus}} {{ill|Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|de|pBM 47.218.50}} concerning Mesqet sanctuariesJean-Claude Goyon: ''Confirmation du pouvoir royal au nouvel an. [Brooklyn Museum 47.218.50]. Planches'' (= ''Bibliothèque d'Étude.'' [BdE] Band 52). Institut Francais d'Archéologie Orientale (IFAO), Kairo 1972, S. 93–94. aligned with newer interpretations that identified Qenqenet explicitly with the eastern and western horizons. The {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch}} clarified its assignment to the gateway regions of the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat}}, just above the horizon. In the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Book of the Dead|de|Ägyptisches Totenbuch}}, Qenqenet is described in detail: "When Ra sets in Qenqenet, he can do anything there, just as one acts on the {{ill|Island of Flames|de|Flammeninsel}}," which directly relates to the winding Duat watercourse {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha}} and the fields of {{ill|Aaru|de|Sechet-iaru|kk=Aaru|lt=Sekhet-iaru}}." {{Quote|{{ill|Horus|de|Horus}}, the {{ill|Falcon|de|Falken}} 1,000 {{ill|Meh|de|Meh}} long, rises and sets in Qenqenet, his birthplace and waterways. O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in {{ill|Sekhet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru}}), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|de|Bertha Porter}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind Moss}}: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Qenqenet-qq]] [[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:qq-Qenqenet-qq]] [[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Qenqenet]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace ]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]] [[de:Qenqenet]]

However, further studies of other texts dismissed this hypothesis, as other evidence clearly referred to it as a "transitional region of the {{ill|Sky|de|Himmel (planetär)}} and the {{ill|Stars|de|Stern}}." Extensive studies by {{ill|Harco Willems|de|Harco Willems}},Harco Willems: ''The coffin of Heqata (Cairo JdE 36418): A case study of Egyptian funerary culture of the Early Middle Kingdom.'' Peeters, Leuven 1996, ISBN 90-6831-769-5, S. 262–270. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss}},Rolf Krauss: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5, S. 254–255. and {{ill|Arno Egberts|de|Arno Egberts}}Arno Egberts: ''In quest of meaning: A study of the ancient Egyptian rites of consecrating the meret chests and driving the calves.'' Nederlands Institut voor het Nabije Osten, Leiden 1995, ISBN 90-6258-208-7, S. 292–293. confined Qenqenet to the horizon's vicinity. Analysis of {{ill|Papyrus|de|Papyrus}} {{ill|Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|de|pBM 47.218.50}} concerning Mesqet sanctuariesJean-Claude Goyon: ''Confirmation du pouvoir royal au nouvel an. [Brooklyn Museum 47.218.50]. Planches'' (= ''Bibliothèque d'Étude.'' [BdE] Band 52). Institut Francais d'Archéologie Orientale (IFAO), Kairo 1972, S. 93–94. aligned with newer interpretations that identified Qenqenet explicitly with the eastern and western horizons. The {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch}} clarified its assignment to the gateway regions of the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat}}, just above the horizon. In the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Book of the Dead|de|Ägyptisches Totenbuch}}, Qenqenet is described in detail: "When Ra sets in Qenqenet, he can do anything there, just as one acts on the {{ill|Island of Flames|de|Flammeninsel}}," which directly relates to the winding Duat watercourse {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha}} and the fields of {{ill|Aaru|de|Sechet-iaru|kk=Aaru|lt=Sekhet-iaru}}." {{Quote|{{ill|Horus|de|Horus}}, the {{ill|Falcon|de|Falken}} 1,000 {{ill|Meh|de|Meh}} long, rises and sets in Qenqenet, his birthplace and waterways. O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in {{ill|Sekhet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru}}), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|de|Bertha Porter}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind Moss}}: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Qenqenet-qq]] [[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Qenqenet]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Qenqenet-qq]] [[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Qenqenet]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace ]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]] [[de:Qenqenet]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = N29:N35-N29:N35-X1 |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Qenqenet
(Qen qenet)
''Qn qn.t'' }} '''Qenqenet''' refers to the celestial region just above the {{ill|Horizon|de|Horizont}} in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie}} and {{ill|Astronomy in Ancient Egypt|de|Astronomie im Alten Ägypten}}. Specifically, it contrasts with the {{ill|Mesqet|de|Mesqet}} and denotes the entrance area to the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat}}. == Background == [[File:Mock mirage of the setting sun.jpg|mini|Qenqenet zone: Sunset over the horizon]] The Qenqenet is the place where {{ill|Ra (deity)|de|Re (ägyptische Mythologie)}}, {{ill|Horus|de|Horus}}, and the {{ill|Baktiu|lt=Decan stars|de|Dekan-Stern}} reside at the beginning of {{ill|Sunset|de|Sonnenuntergang}}, just before they descend fully below the horizon. Mythologically, Qenqenet is therefore known as "the place before {{ill|Reincarnation|de|Wiedergeburt}}." The location of Qenqenet was long debated among {{ill|Egyptologists|de|Ägyptologe}}, as many texts also refer to it as "a region in the sky and the Duat." Qenqenet plays an important role in the {{ill|Pyramid Texts|de|Pyramidentexte}}. Scholars such as {{ill|Kurt Sethe|de|Kurt Sethe}},Kurt Sethe: ''Die altägyptischen Pyramidentexte. Nach den Papierabdrücken und Photographien des Berliner Museums. 1, Text, 1. Hälfte, Spruch 1-468 (Pyr. 1-905).'' Olms, Hildesheim 2001 (Nachdruck von 1908), ISBN 3-487-02593-0, S. 315. {{ill|Arielle Kozloff|de|Arielle Kozloff}},Arielle Kozloff et al.: ''Egypt’s dazzling sun: Amenhotep III and his world.'' Cleveland Museum of Art in cooperation with Indiana University Press, Cleveland 1992, ISBN 0-940717-16-6, S. 336–337. and {{ill|Ronald Wells|de|Ronald Wells}}Ronald Wells: ''The Mythology of Nut and the Birth of Ra.'' In: ''Studien zur altägyptischen Kultur.'' Band 19, 1992, S. 305–321. proposed that it could be the {{ill|Milky Way|de|Milchstraße}}. However, further studies of other texts dismissed this hypothesis, as other evidence clearly referred to it as a "transitional region of the {{ill|Sky|de|Himmel (planetär)}} and the {{ill|Stars|de|Stern}}." Extensive studies by {{ill|Harco Willems|de|Harco Willems}},Harco Willems: ''The coffin of Heqata (Cairo JdE 36418): A case study of Egyptian funerary culture of the Early Middle Kingdom.'' Peeters, Leuven 1996, ISBN 90-6831-769-5, S. 262–270. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss}},Rolf Krauss: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5, S. 254–255. and {{ill|Arno Egberts|de|Arno Egberts}}Arno Egberts: ''In quest of meaning: A study of the ancient Egyptian rites of consecrating the meret chests and driving the calves.'' Nederlands Institut voor het Nabije Osten, Leiden 1995, ISBN 90-6258-208-7, S. 292–293. confined Qenqenet to the horizon's vicinity. Analysis of {{ill|Papyrus|de|Papyrus}} {{ill|Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|de|pBM 47.218.50}} concerning Mesqet sanctuariesJean-Claude Goyon: ''Confirmation du pouvoir royal au nouvel an. [Brooklyn Museum 47.218.50]. Planches'' (= ''Bibliothèque d'Étude.'' [BdE] Band 52). Institut Francais d'Archéologie Orientale (IFAO), Kairo 1972, S. 93–94. aligned with newer interpretations that identified Qenqenet explicitly with the eastern and western horizons. The {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch}} clarified its assignment to the gateway regions of the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat}}, just above the horizon. In the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Book of the Dead|de|Ägyptisches Totenbuch}}, Qenqenet is described in detail: "When Ra sets in Qenqenet, he can do anything there, just as one acts on the {{ill|Island of Flames|de|Flammeninsel}}," which directly relates to the winding Duat watercourse {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha}} and the fields of {{ill|Aaru|de|Sechet-iaru|kk=Aaru|lt=Sekhet-iaru}}." {{Quote|{{ill|Horus|de|Horus}}, the {{ill|Falcon|de|Falken}} 1,000 {{ill|Meh|de|Meh}} long, rises and sets in Qenqenet, his birthplace and waterways. O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in {{ill|Aaru|lt=Sekhet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru}}), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|de|Bertha Porter}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind Moss}}: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Qenqenet-qq]] [[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Qenqenet]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq-Qenqenet-qq]] [[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Qenqenet]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace ]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]] [[de:Qenqenet]]

The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Qenqenet]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Qenqenet]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace ]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]] [[de:Qenqenet]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = N29:N35-N29:N35-X1 |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Qenqenet
(Qen qenet)
''Qn qn.t'' }} '''Qenqenet''' refers to the celestial region just above the {{ill|Horizon|de|Horizont}} in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie}} and {{ill|Astronomy in Ancient Egypt|de|Astronomie im Alten Ägypten}}. Specifically, it contrasts with the {{ill|Mesqet|de|Mesqet}} and denotes the entrance area to the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat}}. == Background == [[File:Mock mirage of the setting sun.jpg|mini|Qenqenet zone: Sunset over the horizon]] The Qenqenet is the place where {{ill|Ra (deity)|de|Re (ägyptische Mythologie)}}, {{ill|Horus|de|Horus}}, and the {{ill|Baktiu|lt=Decan stars|de|Dekan-Stern}} reside at the beginning of {{ill|Sunset|de|Sonnenuntergang}}, just before they descend fully below the horizon. Mythologically, Qenqenet is therefore known as "the place before {{ill|Reincarnation|de|Wiedergeburt}}." The location of Qenqenet was long debated among {{ill|Egyptologists|de|Ägyptologe}}, as many texts also refer to it as "a region in the sky and the Duat." Qenqenet plays an important role in the {{ill|Pyramid Texts|de|Pyramidentexte}}. Scholars such as {{ill|Kurt Sethe|de|Kurt Sethe}},Kurt Sethe: ''Die altägyptischen Pyramidentexte. Nach den Papierabdrücken und Photographien des Berliner Museums. 1, Text, 1. Hälfte, Spruch 1-468 (Pyr. 1-905).'' Olms, Hildesheim 2001 (Nachdruck von 1908), ISBN 3-487-02593-0, S. 315. {{ill|Arielle Kozloff|de|Arielle Kozloff}},Arielle Kozloff et al.: ''Egypt’s dazzling sun: Amenhotep III and his world.'' Cleveland Museum of Art in cooperation with Indiana University Press, Cleveland 1992, ISBN 0-940717-16-6, S. 336–337. and {{ill|Ronald Wells|de|Ronald Wells}}Ronald Wells: ''The Mythology of Nut and the Birth of Ra.'' In: ''Studien zur altägyptischen Kultur.'' Band 19, 1992, S. 305–321. proposed that it could be the {{ill|Milky Way|de|Milchstraße}}. However, further studies of other texts dismissed this hypothesis, as other evidence clearly referred to it as a "transitional region of the {{ill|Sky|de|Himmel (planetär)}} and the {{ill|Stars|de|Stern}}." Extensive studies by {{ill|Harco Willems|de|Harco Willems}},Harco Willems: ''The coffin of Heqata (Cairo JdE 36418): A case study of Egyptian funerary culture of the Early Middle Kingdom.'' Peeters, Leuven 1996, ISBN 90-6831-769-5, S. 262–270. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss}},Rolf Krauss: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5, S. 254–255. and {{ill|Arno Egberts|de|Arno Egberts}}Arno Egberts: ''In quest of meaning: A study of the ancient Egyptian rites of consecrating the meret chests and driving the calves.'' Nederlands Institut voor het Nabije Osten, Leiden 1995, ISBN 90-6258-208-7, S. 292–293. confined Qenqenet to the horizon's vicinity. Analysis of {{ill|Papyrus|de|Papyrus}} {{ill|Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|de|pBM 47.218.50}} concerning Mesqet sanctuariesJean-Claude Goyon: ''Confirmation du pouvoir royal au nouvel an. [Brooklyn Museum 47.218.50]. Planches'' (= ''Bibliothèque d'Étude.'' [BdE] Band 52). Institut Francais d'Archéologie Orientale (IFAO), Kairo 1972, S. 93–94. aligned with newer interpretations that identified Qenqenet explicitly with the eastern and western horizons. The {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch}} clarified its assignment to the gateway regions of the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat}}, just above the horizon. In the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Book of the Dead|de|Ägyptisches Totenbuch}}, Qenqenet is described in detail: "When Ra sets in Qenqenet, he can do anything there, just as one acts on the {{ill|Island of Flames|de|Flammeninsel}}," which directly relates to the winding Duat watercourse {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha}} and the fields of {{ill|Aaru|de|Sechet-iaru|kk=Aaru|lt=Sekhet-iaru}}." {{Quote|{{ill|Horus|de|Horus}}, the {{ill|Falcon|de|Falken}} 1,000 {{ill|Meh|de|Meh}} long, rises and sets in Qenqenet, his birthplace and waterways. O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in {{ill|Aaru|lt=Sekhet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru}}), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|de|Bertha Porter}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind Moss}}: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Qenqenet]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated start]][[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Qenqenet]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace ]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]] [[de:Qenqenet]]

Nach den Papierabdrücken und Photographien des Berliner Museums. 1, Text, 1. Hälfte, Spruch 1-468 (Pyr. 1-905).'' Olms, Hildesheim 2001 (Nachdruck von 1908), ISBN 3-487-02593-0, S. 315. {{ill|Arielle Kozloff|de|Arielle Kozloff}},Arielle Kozloff et al.: ''Egypt’s dazzling sun: Amenhotep III and his world.'' Cleveland Museum of Art in cooperation with Indiana University Press, Cleveland 1992, ISBN 0-940717-16-6, S. 336–337. and {{ill|Ronald Wells|de|Ronald Wells}}Ronald Wells: ''The Mythology of Nut and the Birth of Ra.'' In: ''Studien zur altägyptischen Kultur.'' Band 19, 1992, S. 305–321. proposed that it could be the {{ill|Milky Way|de|Milchstraße}}. However, further studies of other texts dismissed this hypothesis, as other evidence clearly referred to it as a "transitional region of the {{ill|Sky|de|Himmel (planetär)}} and the {{ill|Stars|de|Stern}}." Extensive studies by {{ill|Harco Willems|de|Harco Willems}},Harco Willems: ''The coffin of Heqata (Cairo JdE 36418): A case study of Egyptian funerary culture of the Early Middle Kingdom.'' Peeters, Leuven 1996, ISBN 90-6831-769-5, S. 262–270. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss}},Rolf Krauss: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5, S. 254–255. and {{ill|Arno Egberts|de|Arno Egberts}}Arno Egberts: ''In quest of meaning: A study of the ancient Egyptian rites of consecrating the meret chests and driving the calves.'' Nederlands Institut voor het Nabije Osten, Leiden 1995, ISBN 90-6258-208-7, S. 292–293. confined Qenqenet to the horizon's vicinity. Analysis of {{ill|Papyrus|de|Papyrus}} {{ill|Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|de|pBM 47.218.50}} concerning Mesqet sanctuariesJean-Claude Goyon: ''Confirmation du pouvoir royal au nouvel an. [Brooklyn Museum 47.218.50]. Planches'' (= ''Bibliothèque d'Étude.'' [BdE] Band 52). Institut Francais d'Archéologie Orientale (IFAO), Kairo 1972, S. 93–94. aligned with newer interpretations that identified Qenqenet explicitly with the eastern and western horizons. The {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch}} clarified its assignment to the gateway regions of the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat}}, just above the horizon. In the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Book of the Dead|de|Ägyptisches Totenbuch}}, Qenqenet is described in detail: "When Ra sets in Qenqenet, he can do anything there, just as one acts on the {{ill|Island of Flames|de|Flammeninsel}}," which directly relates to the winding Duat watercourse {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha}} and the fields of {{ill|Aaru|de|Sechet-iaru|kk=Aaru|lt=Sekhet-iaru}}." {{Quote|{{ill|Horus|de|Horus}}, the {{ill|Falcon|de|Falken}} 1,000 {{ill|Meh|de|Meh}} long, rises and sets in Qenqenet, his birthplace and waterways. O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in {{ill|Aaru|lt=Sekhet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru}}), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|de|Bertha Porter}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind Moss}}: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Qenqenet]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated close]] [[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Qenqenet]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace ]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]] [[de:Qenqenet]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = N29:N35-N29:N35-X1 |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Qenqenet
(Qen qenet)
''Qn qn.t'' }} '''Qenqenet''' refers to the celestial region just above the {{ill|Horizon|de|Horizont}} in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie}} and {{ill|Astronomy in Ancient Egypt|de|Astronomie im Alten Ägypten}}. Specifically, it contrasts with the {{ill|Mesqet|de|Mesqet}} and denotes the entrance area to the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat}}. == Background == [[File:Mock mirage of the setting sun.jpg|mini|Qenqenet zone: Sunset over the horizon]] The Qenqenet is the place where {{ill|Ra (deity)|de|Re (ägyptische Mythologie)}}, {{ill|Horus|de|Horus}}, and the {{ill|Baktiu|lt=Decan stars|de|Dekan-Stern}} reside at the beginning of {{ill|Sunset|de|Sonnenuntergang}}, just before they descend fully below the horizon. Mythologically, Qenqenet is therefore known as "the place before {{ill|Reincarnation|de|Wiedergeburt}}." The location of Qenqenet was long debated among {{ill|Egyptologists|de|Ägyptologe}}, as many texts also refer to it as "a region in the sky and the Duat." Qenqenet plays an important role in the {{ill|Pyramid Texts|de|Pyramidentexte}}. Scholars such as {{ill|Kurt Sethe|de|Kurt Sethe}},Kurt Sethe: ''Die altägyptischen Pyramidentexte. Nach den Papierabdrücken und Photographien des Berliner Museums. 1, Text, 1. Hälfte, Spruch 1-468 (Pyr. 1-905).'' Olms, Hildesheim 2001 (Nachdruck von 1908), ISBN 3-487-02593-0, S. 315. {{ill|Arielle Kozloff|de|Arielle Kozloff}},Arielle Kozloff et al.: ''Egypt’s dazzling sun: Amenhotep III and his world.'' Cleveland Museum of Art in cooperation with Indiana University Press, Cleveland 1992, ISBN 0-940717-16-6, S. 336–337. and {{ill|Ronald Wells|de|Ronald Wells}}Ronald Wells: ''The Mythology of Nut and the Birth of Ra.'' In: ''Studien zur altägyptischen Kultur.'' Band 19, 1992, S. 305–321. proposed that it could be the {{ill|Milky Way|de|Milchstraße}}. However, further studies of other texts dismissed this hypothesis, as other evidence clearly referred to it as a "transitional region of the {{ill|Sky|de|Himmel (planetär)}} and the {{ill|Stars|de|Stern}}." Extensive studies by {{ill|Harco Willems|de|Harco Willems}},Harco Willems: ''The coffin of Heqata (Cairo JdE 36418): A case study of Egyptian funerary culture of the Early Middle Kingdom.'' Peeters, Leuven 1996, ISBN 90-6831-769-5, S. 262–270. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss}},Rolf Krauss: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5, S. 254–255. and {{ill|Arno Egberts|de|Arno Egberts}}Arno Egberts: ''In quest of meaning: A study of the ancient Egyptian rites of consecrating the meret chests and driving the calves.'' Nederlands Institut voor het Nabije Osten, Leiden 1995, ISBN 90-6258-208-7, S. 292–293. confined Qenqenet to the horizon's vicinity. Analysis of {{ill|Papyrus|de|Papyrus}} {{ill|Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|de|pBM 47.218.50}} concerning Mesqet sanctuariesJean-Claude Goyon: ''Confirmation du pouvoir royal au nouvel an. [Brooklyn Museum 47.218.50]. Planches'' (= ''Bibliothèque d'Étude.'' [BdE] Band 52). Institut Francais d'Archéologie Orientale (IFAO), Kairo 1972, S. 93–94. aligned with newer interpretations that identified Qenqenet explicitly with the eastern and western horizons. The {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch}} clarified its assignment to the gateway regions of the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat}}, just above the horizon. In the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Book of the Dead|de|Ägyptisches Totenbuch}}, Qenqenet is described in detail: "When Ra sets in Qenqenet, he can do anything there, just as one acts on the {{ill|Island of Flames|de|Flammeninsel}}," which directly relates to the winding Duat watercourse {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha}} and the fields of {{ill|Aaru|de|Sechet-iaru|kk=Aaru|lt=Sekhet-iaru}}." {{Quote|{{ill|Horus|de|Horus}}, the {{ill|Falcon|de|Falken}} 1,000 {{ill|Meh|de|Meh}} long, rises and sets in Qenqenet, his birthplace and waterways. O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in {{ill|Aaru|lt=Sekhet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru}}), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|de|Bertha Porter}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind Moss}}: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Qenqenet]] [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 completion categories]] [[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]][[Category:Qenqenet]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Qenqenet]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace ]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]] [[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]] [[de:Qenqenet]]
