Qenqenet plays an important role in the {{ill|Pyramid Texts|de|Pyramidentexte|12=draft|12=draft}}. Scholars such as {{ill|Kurt Sethe|de|Kurt Sethe|12=draft|12=draft}},Kurt Sethe: ''Die altägyptischen Pyramidentexte. Nach den Papierabdrücken und Photographien des Berliner Museums. 1, Text, 1. Hälfte, Spruch 1-468 (Pyr. 1-905).'' Olms, Hildesheim 2001 (Nachdruck von 1908), ISBN 3-487-02593-0, S. 315. {{ill|Arielle Kozloff|de|Arielle Kozloff|12=draft|12=draft}},Arielle Kozloff et al.: ''Egypt’s dazzling sun: Amenhotep III and his world.'' Cleveland Museum of Art in cooperation with Indiana University Press, Cleveland 1992, ISBN 0-940717-16-6, S. 336–337. and {{ill|Ronald Wells|de|Ronald Wells|12=draft|12=draft}}Ronald Wells: ''The Mythology of Nut and the Birth of Ra.'' In: ''Studien zur altägyptischen Kultur.'' Band 19, 1992, S. 305–321. proposed that it could be the {{ill|Milky Way|de|Milchstraße|12=draft|12=draft}}. However, further studies of other texts dismissed this hypothesis, as other evidence clearly referred to it as a "transitional region of the {{ill|Sky|de|Himmel (planetär)|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Stars|de|Stern|12=draft|12=draft}}." Extensive studies by {{ill|Harco Willems|de|Harco Willems|12=draft|12=draft}},Harco Willems: ''The coffin of Heqata (Cairo JdE 36418): A case study of Egyptian funerary culture of the Early Middle Kingdom.'' Peeters, Leuven 1996, ISBN 90-6831-769-5, S. 262–270. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=draft|12=draft}},Rolf Krauss: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5, S. 254–255. and {{ill|Arno Egberts|de|Arno Egberts|12=draft|12=draft}}Arno Egberts: ''In quest of meaning: A study of the ancient Egyptian rites of consecrating the meret chests and driving the calves.'' Nederlands Institut voor het Nabije Osten, Leiden 1995, ISBN 90-6258-208-7, S. 292–293. confined Qenqenet to the horizon's vicinity. Analysis of {{ill|Papyrus|de|Papyrus|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|de|pBM 47.218.50|12=draft|12=draft}} concerning Mesqet sanctuariesJean-Claude Goyon: ''Confirmation du pouvoir royal au nouvel an. [Brooklyn Museum 47.218.50]. Planches'' (= ''Bibliothèque d'Étude.'' [BdE] Band 52). Institut Francais d'Archéologie Orientale (IFAO), Kairo 1972, S. 93–94. aligned with newer interpretations that identified Qenqenet explicitly with the eastern and western horizons. The {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch|12=draft|12=draft}} clarified its assignment to the gateway regions of the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|12=draft|12=draft}}, just above the horizon. In the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Book of the Dead|de|Ägyptisches Totenbuch|12=draft|12=draft}}, Qenqenet is described in detail: "When Ra sets in Qenqenet, he can do anything there, just as one acts on the {{ill|Island of Flames|de|Flammeninsel|12=draft|12=draft}}," which directly relates to the winding Duat watercourse {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|12=draft|12=draft}} and the fields of {{ill|Aaru|de|Sechet-iaru|kk=Aaru|lt=Sekhet-iaru|12=draft|12=draft}}." {{Quote|{{ill|Horus|de|Horus|12=draft|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Falcon|de|Falken|12=draft|12=draft}} 1,000 {{ill|Meh|de|Meh|12=draft|12=draft}} long, rises and sets in Qenqenet, his birthplace and waterways. O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in {{ill|Aaru|lt=Sekhet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru|12=draft|12=draft}}), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|de|Bertha Porter|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind Moss|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Qenqenet]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace ]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Qenqenet]] [[de:Qenqenet]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = N29:N35-N29:N35-X1 |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Qenqenet
(Qen qenet)
''Qn qn.t'' }} '''Qenqenet''' refers to the celestial region just above the {{ill|Horizon|de|Horizont|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Astronomy in Ancient Egypt|de|Astronomie im Alten Ägypten|12=draft|12=draft}}. Specifically, it contrasts with the {{ill|Mesqet|de|Mesqet|12=draft|12=draft}} and denotes the entrance area to the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Background == [[File:Mock_mirage_of_the_setting_sun.jpg|alt=Qenqenet zone: Sunset over the horizon|thumb]] The Qenqenet is the place where {{ill|Ra (deity)|de|Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Ra|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|Baktiu|lt=Decan stars|de|Dekan-Stern|12=draft|12=draft}} reside at the beginning of {{ill|Sunset|de|Sonnenuntergang|12=draft|12=draft}}, just before they descend fully below the horizon. Mythologically, Qenqenet is therefore known as "the place before {{ill|Reincarnation|de|Wiedergeburt|12=draft|12=draft}}." The location of Qenqenet was long debated among {{ill|Egyptologists|de|Ägyptologe|12=draft|12=draft}}, as many texts also refer to it as "a region in the sky and the Duat." Qenqenet plays an important role in the {{ill|Pyramid Texts|de|Pyramidentexte|12=draft|12=draft}}. Scholars such as {{ill|Kurt Sethe|de|Kurt Sethe|12=draft|12=draft}},Kurt Sethe: ''Die altägyptischen Pyramidentexte. Nach den Papierabdrücken und Photographien des Berliner Museums. 1, Text, 1. Hälfte, Spruch 1-468 (Pyr. 1-905).'' Olms, Hildesheim 2001 (Nachdruck von 1908), ISBN 3-487-02593-0, S. 315. {{ill|Arielle Kozloff|de|Arielle Kozloff|12=draft|12=draft}},Arielle Kozloff et al.: ''Egypt’s dazzling sun: Amenhotep III and his world.'' Cleveland Museum of Art in cooperation with Indiana University Press, Cleveland 1992, ISBN 0-940717-16-6, S. 336–337. and {{ill|Ronald Wells|de|Ronald Wells|12=draft|12=draft}}Ronald Wells: ''The Mythology of Nut and the Birth of Ra.'' In: ''Studien zur altägyptischen Kultur.'' Band 19, 1992, S. 305–321. proposed that it could be the {{ill|Milky Way|de|Milchstraße|12=draft|12=draft}}. However, further studies of other texts dismissed this hypothesis, as other evidence clearly referred to it as a "transitional region of the {{ill|Sky|de|Himmel (planetär)|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Stars|de|Stern|12=draft|12=draft}}." Extensive studies by {{ill|Harco Willems|de|Harco Willems|12=draft|12=draft}},Harco Willems: ''The coffin of Heqata (Cairo JdE 36418): A case study of Egyptian funerary culture of the Early Middle Kingdom.'' Peeters, Leuven 1996, ISBN 90-6831-769-5, S. 262–270. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=draft|12=draft}},Rolf Krauss: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5, S. 254–255. and {{ill|Arno Egberts|de|Arno Egberts|12=draft|12=draft}}Arno Egberts: ''In quest of meaning: A study of the ancient Egyptian rites of consecrating the meret chests and driving the calves.'' Nederlands Institut voor het Nabije Osten, Leiden 1995, ISBN 90-6258-208-7, S. 292–293. confined Qenqenet to the horizon's vicinity. Analysis of {{ill|Papyrus|de|Papyrus|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|de|pBM 47.218.50|12=draft|12=draft}} concerning Mesqet sanctuariesJean-Claude Goyon: ''Confirmation du pouvoir royal au nouvel an. [Brooklyn Museum 47.218.50]. Planches'' (= ''Bibliothèque d'Étude.'' [BdE] Band 52). Institut Francais d'Archéologie Orientale (IFAO), Kairo 1972, S. 93–94. aligned with newer interpretations that identified Qenqenet explicitly with the eastern and western horizons. The {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch|12=draft|12=draft}} clarified its assignment to the gateway regions of the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|12=draft|12=draft}}, just above the horizon. In the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Book of the Dead|de|Ägyptisches Totenbuch|12=draft|12=draft}}, Qenqenet is described in detail: "When Ra sets in Qenqenet, he can do anything there, just as one acts on the {{ill|Island of Flames|de|Flammeninsel|12=draft|12=draft}}," which directly relates to the winding Duat watercourse {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|12=draft|12=draft}} and the fields of {{ill|Aaru|de|Sechet-iaru|kk=Aaru|lt=Sekhet-iaru|12=draft|12=draft}}." {{Quote|{{ill|Horus|de|Horus|12=draft|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Falcon|de|Falken|12=draft|12=draft}} 1,000 {{ill|Meh|de|Meh|12=draft|12=draft}} long, rises and sets in Qenqenet, his birthplace and waterways. O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in {{ill|Aaru|lt=Sekhet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru|12=draft|12=draft}}), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in {{ill|Aaru|lt=Sekhet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru|12=draft|12=draft}}), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|de|Bertha Porter|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind Moss|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == {{draft categories| [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Qenqenet]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace ]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Qenqenet]] [[de:Qenqenet]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = N29:N35-N29:N35-X1 |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Qenqenet
(Qen qenet)
''Qn qn.t'' }} '''Qenqenet''' refers to the celestial region just above the {{ill|Horizon|de|Horizont|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Astronomy in Ancient Egypt|de|Astronomie im Alten Ägypten|12=draft|12=draft}}. Specifically, it contrasts with the {{ill|Mesqet|de|Mesqet|12=draft|12=draft}} and denotes the entrance area to the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Background == [[File:Mock_mirage_of_the_setting_sun.jpg|alt=Qenqenet zone: Sunset over the horizon|thumb]] The Qenqenet is the place where {{ill|Ra (deity)|de|Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Ra|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|Baktiu|lt=Decan stars|de|Dekan-Stern|12=draft|12=draft}} reside at the beginning of {{ill|Sunset|de|Sonnenuntergang|12=draft|12=draft}}, just before they descend fully below the horizon. Mythologically, Qenqenet is therefore known as "the place before {{ill|Reincarnation|de|Wiedergeburt|12=draft|12=draft}}." The location of Qenqenet was long debated among {{ill|Egyptologists|de|Ägyptologe|12=draft|12=draft}}, as many texts also refer to it as "a region in the sky and the Duat." Qenqenet plays an important role in the {{ill|Pyramid Texts|de|Pyramidentexte|12=draft|12=draft}}. Scholars such as {{ill|Kurt Sethe|de|Kurt Sethe|12=draft|12=draft}},Kurt Sethe: ''Die altägyptischen Pyramidentexte. Nach den Papierabdrücken und Photographien des Berliner Museums. 1, Text, 1. Hälfte, Spruch 1-468 (Pyr. 1-905).'' Olms, Hildesheim 2001 (Nachdruck von 1908), ISBN 3-487-02593-0, S. 315. {{ill|Arielle Kozloff|de|Arielle Kozloff|12=draft|12=draft}},Arielle Kozloff et al.: ''Egypt’s dazzling sun: Amenhotep III and his world.'' Cleveland Museum of Art in cooperation with Indiana University Press, Cleveland 1992, ISBN 0-940717-16-6, S. 336–337. and {{ill|Ronald Wells|de|Ronald Wells|12=draft|12=draft}}Ronald Wells: ''The Mythology of Nut and the Birth of Ra.'' In: ''Studien zur altägyptischen Kultur.'' Band 19, 1992, S. 305–321. proposed that it could be the {{ill|Milky Way|de|Milchstraße|12=draft|12=draft}}. However, further studies of other texts dismissed this hypothesis, as other evidence clearly referred to it as a "transitional region of the {{ill|Sky|de|Himmel (planetär)|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Stars|de|Stern|12=draft|12=draft}}." Extensive studies by {{ill|Harco Willems|de|Harco Willems|12=draft|12=draft}},Harco Willems: ''The coffin of Heqata (Cairo JdE 36418): A case study of Egyptian funerary culture of the Early Middle Kingdom.'' Peeters, Leuven 1996, ISBN 90-6831-769-5, S. 262–270. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=draft|12=draft}},Rolf Krauss: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5, S. 254–255. and {{ill|Arno Egberts|de|Arno Egberts|12=draft|12=draft}}Arno Egberts: ''In quest of meaning: A study of the ancient Egyptian rites of consecrating the meret chests and driving the calves.'' Nederlands Institut voor het Nabije Osten, Leiden 1995, ISBN 90-6258-208-7, S. 292–293. confined Qenqenet to the horizon's vicinity. Analysis of {{ill|Papyrus|de|Papyrus|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|de|pBM 47.218.50|12=draft|12=draft}} concerning Mesqet sanctuariesJean-Claude Goyon: ''Confirmation du pouvoir royal au nouvel an. [Brooklyn Museum 47.218.50]. Planches'' (= ''Bibliothèque d'Étude.'' [BdE] Band 52). Institut Francais d'Archéologie Orientale (IFAO), Kairo 1972, S. 93–94. aligned with newer interpretations that identified Qenqenet explicitly with the eastern and western horizons. The {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch|12=draft|12=draft}} clarified its assignment to the gateway regions of the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|12=draft|12=draft}}, just above the horizon. In the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Book of the Dead|de|Ägyptisches Totenbuch|12=draft|12=draft}}, Qenqenet is described in detail: "When Ra sets in Qenqenet, he can do anything there, just as one acts on the {{ill|Island of Flames|de|Flammeninsel|12=draft|12=draft}}," which directly relates to the winding Duat watercourse {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|12=draft|12=draft}} and the fields of {{ill|Aaru|de|Sechet-iaru|kk=Aaru|lt=Sekhet-iaru|12=draft|12=draft}}." {{Quote|{{ill|Horus|de|Horus|12=draft|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Falcon|de|Falken|12=draft|12=draft}} 1,000 {{ill|Meh|de|Meh|12=draft|12=draft}} long, rises and sets in Qenqenet, his birthplace and waterways. O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in {{ill|Aaru|lt=Sekhet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru|12=draft|12=draft}}), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|de|Bertha Porter|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind Moss|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == {{draft categories| [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Qenqenet]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace ]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Qenqenet]] [[de:Qenqenet]]

In: ''Studien zur altägyptischen Kultur.'' Band 19, 1992, S. 305–321. proposed that it could be the {{ill|Milky Way|de|Milchstraße|12=draft|12=draft}}. However, further studies of other texts dismissed this hypothesis, as other evidence clearly referred to it as a "transitional region of the {{ill|Sky|de|Himmel (planetär)|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Stars|de|Stern|12=draft|12=draft}}." Extensive studies by {{ill|Harco Willems|de|Harco Willems|12=draft|12=draft}},Harco Willems: ''The coffin of Heqata (Cairo JdE 36418): A case study of Egyptian funerary culture of the Early Middle Kingdom.'' Peeters, Leuven 1996, ISBN 90-6831-769-5, S. 262–270. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=draft|12=draft}},Rolf Krauss: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5, S. 254–255. and {{ill|Arno Egberts|de|Arno Egberts|12=draft|12=draft}}Arno Egberts: ''In quest of meaning: A study of the ancient Egyptian rites of consecrating the meret chests and driving the calves.'' Nederlands Institut voor het Nabije Osten, Leiden 1995, ISBN 90-6258-208-7, S. 292–293. confined Qenqenet to the horizon's vicinity. Analysis of {{ill|Papyrus|de|Papyrus|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|de|pBM 47.218.50|12=draft|12=draft}} concerning Mesqet sanctuariesJean-Claude Goyon: ''Confirmation du pouvoir royal au nouvel an. [Brooklyn Museum 47.218.50]. Planches'' (= ''Bibliothèque d'Étude.'' [BdE] Band 52). Institut Francais d'Archéologie Orientale (IFAO), Kairo 1972, S. 93–94. aligned with newer interpretations that identified Qenqenet explicitly with the eastern and western horizons. The {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch|12=draft|12=draft}} clarified its assignment to the gateway regions of the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|12=draft|12=draft}}, just above the horizon. In the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Book of the Dead|de|Ägyptisches Totenbuch|12=draft|12=draft}}, Qenqenet is described in detail: "When Ra sets in Qenqenet, he can do anything there, just as one acts on the {{ill|Island of Flames|de|Flammeninsel|12=draft|12=draft}}," which directly relates to the winding Duat watercourse {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|12=draft|12=draft}} and the fields of {{ill|Aaru|de|Sechet-iaru|kk=Aaru|lt=Sekhet-iaru|12=draft|12=draft}}." {{Quote|{{ill|Horus|de|Horus|12=draft|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Falcon|de|Falken|12=draft|12=draft}} 1,000 {{ill|Meh|de|Meh|12=draft|12=draft}} long, rises and sets in Qenqenet, his birthplace and waterways. O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in {{ill|Aaru|lt=Sekhet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru|12=draft|12=draft}}), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|de|Bertha Porter|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind Moss|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == {{draft categories| [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Qenqenet]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:pages tagged as Automatic wikipedia redirects in namespace ]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Qenqenet]] [[de:Qenqenet]]

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = N29:N35-N29:N35-X1 |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Qenqenet
(Qen qenet)
''Qn qn.t'' }} '''Qenqenet''' refers to the celestial region just above the {{ill|Horizon|de|Horizont|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Astronomy in Ancient Egypt|de|Astronomie im Alten Ägypten|12=draft|12=draft}}. Specifically, it contrasts with the {{ill|Mesqet|de|Mesqet|12=draft|12=draft}} and denotes the entrance area to the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Background == [[File:Mock_mirage_of_the_setting_sun.jpg|alt=Qenqenet zone: Sunset over the horizon|thumb]] The Qenqenet is the place where {{ill|Ra (deity)|de|Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Ra|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|Baktiu|lt=Decan stars|de|Dekan-Stern|12=draft|12=draft}} reside at the beginning of {{ill|Sunset|de|Sonnenuntergang|12=draft|12=draft}}, just before they descend fully below the horizon. Mythologically, Qenqenet is therefore known as "the place before {{ill|Reincarnation|de|Wiedergeburt|12=draft|12=draft}}." The location of Qenqenet was long debated among {{ill|Egyptologists|de|Ägyptologe|12=draft|12=draft}}, as many texts also refer to it as "a region in the sky and the Duat." Qenqenet plays an important role in the {{ill|Pyramid Texts|de|Pyramidentexte|12=draft|12=draft}}. Scholars such as {{ill|Kurt Sethe|de|Kurt Sethe|12=draft|12=draft}},Kurt Sethe: ''Die altägyptischen Pyramidentexte. Nach den Papierabdrücken und Photographien des Berliner Museums. 1, Text, 1. Hälfte, Spruch 1-468 (Pyr. 1-905).'' Olms, Hildesheim 2001 (Nachdruck von 1908), ISBN 3-487-02593-0, S. 315. {{ill|Arielle Kozloff|de|Arielle Kozloff|12=draft|12=draft}},Arielle Kozloff et al.: ''Egypt’s dazzling sun: Amenhotep III and his world.'' Cleveland Museum of Art in cooperation with Indiana University Press, Cleveland 1992, ISBN 0-940717-16-6, S. 336–337. and {{ill|Ronald Wells|de|Ronald Wells|12=draft|12=draft}}Ronald Wells: ''The Mythology of Nut and the Birth of Ra.'' In: ''Studien zur altägyptischen Kultur.'' Band 19, 1992, S. 305–321. proposed that it could be the {{ill|Milky Way|de|Milchstraße|12=draft|12=draft}}. However, further studies of other texts dismissed this hypothesis, as other evidence clearly referred to it as a "transitional region of the {{ill|Sky|de|Himmel (planetär)|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Stars|de|Stern|12=draft|12=draft}}." Extensive studies by {{ill|Harco Willems|de|Harco Willems|12=draft|12=draft}},Harco Willems: ''The coffin of Heqata (Cairo JdE 36418): A case study of Egyptian funerary culture of the Early Middle Kingdom.'' Peeters, Leuven 1996, ISBN 90-6831-769-5, S. 262–270. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=draft|12=draft}},Rolf Krauss: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5, S. 254–255. and {{ill|Arno Egberts|de|Arno Egberts|12=draft|12=draft}}Arno Egberts: ''In quest of meaning: A study of the ancient Egyptian rites of consecrating the meret chests and driving the calves.'' Nederlands Institut voor het Nabije Osten, Leiden 1995, ISBN 90-6258-208-7, S. 292–293. confined Qenqenet to the horizon's vicinity. Analysis of {{ill|Papyrus|de|Papyrus|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|de|pBM 47.218.50|12=draft|12=draft}} concerning Mesqet sanctuariesJean-Claude Goyon: ''Confirmation du pouvoir royal au nouvel an. [Brooklyn Museum 47.218.50]. Planches'' (= ''Bibliothèque d'Étude.'' [BdE] Band 52). Institut Francais d'Archéologie Orientale (IFAO), Kairo 1972, S. 93–94. aligned with newer interpretations that identified Qenqenet explicitly with the eastern and western horizons. The {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch|12=draft|12=draft}} clarified its assignment to the gateway regions of the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|12=draft|12=draft}}, just above the horizon. In the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Book of the Dead|de|Ägyptisches Totenbuch|12=draft|12=draft}}, Qenqenet is described in detail: "When Ra sets in Qenqenet, he can do anything there, just as one acts on the {{ill|Island of Flames|de|Flammeninsel|12=draft|12=draft}}," which directly relates to the winding Duat watercourse {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|12=draft|12=draft}} and the fields of {{ill|Aaru|de|Sechet-iaru|kk=Aaru|lt=Sekhet-iaru|12=draft|12=draft}}." {{Quote|{{ill|Horus|de|Horus|12=draft|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Falcon|de|Falken|12=draft|12=draft}} 1,000 {{ill|Meh|de|Meh|12=draft|12=draft}} long, rises and sets in Qenqenet, his birthplace and waterways. O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in {{ill|Aaru|lt=Sekhet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru|12=draft|12=draft}}), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|12=draft|12=draft}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|de|Bertha Porter|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind Moss|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=draft|12=draft}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == {{draft categories| [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Qenqenet]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Qenqenet]] [[de:Qenqenet]]

revision.comment: /* Literatur */ [[Benutzer:Aka/Tippfehler entfernt|Tippfehler entfernt]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = N29:N35-N29:N35-X1 |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Qenqenet
(Qen qenet)
''Qn qn.t'' }} '''Qenqenet''' bezeichnet in der [[Ägyptische Mythologie|ägyptischen Mythologie]] und [[Astronomie im Alten Ägypten|Astronomie]] die Himmelsregion knapp über dem [[Horizont]] und bezieht sich speziell im Gegensatz zur [[Mesqet]] auf den Eingangsbereich in die [[Duat]]. == Hintergrund == [[Datei:Mock mirage of the setting sun.jpg|mini|Qenqenet-Zone: Sonnenuntergang über dem Horizont]] Die Qenqenet ist der Ort, an welchem sich [[Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|Re]], [[Horus]] und die [[Dekan-Stern]]e im Moment des beginnenden [[Sonnenuntergang]]es beziehungsweise kurz vor ihrem Tod aufhalten, bevor sie gänzlich unter den Horizont sinken. Die Qenqenet ist [[Mythologie|mythologisch]] daher auch „der Ort vor der [[Reinkarnation|Wiedergeburt]]“. Die Lokalität des Qenqenet war unter den [[Ägyptologe]]n lange umstritten, da dieser Bereich in vielen Texten auch als „Gegend am Himmel und in der Duat“ belegt ist. Die Qenqenet spielt schon in den [[Pyramidentexte]]n eine wichtige Rolle. So nahm beispielsweise [[Kurt Sethe]] an,Kurt Sethe: ''Die altägyptischen Pyramidentexte. Nach den Papierabdrücken und Photographien des Berliner Museums. 1, Text, 1. Hälfte, Spruch 1-468 (Pyr. 1-905).'' Olms, Hildesheim 2001 (Nachdruck von 1908), ISBN 3-487-02593-0, S. 315. [[Arielle Kozloff]]Arielle Kozloff u. a.: ''Egypt’s dazzling sun: Amenhotep III and his world.'' Cleveland Museum of Art in cooperation with Indiana University Press, Cleveland 1992, ISBN 0-940717-16-6, S. 336–337. und [[Ronald Wells]],Ronald Wells: ''The Mythology of Nut and the Birth of Ra.'' In: ''Studien zur altägyptischen Kultur.'' Band 19, 1992, S. 305–321. dass es sich um die [[Milchstraße]] handeln könnte. Nach weiteren Untersuchungen anderer Texte konnte dieser Vorschlag nicht mehr in die engere Wahl gezogen werden, da sich die anderen Belege deutlich auf eine „Durchgangsregion des [[Himmel (planetär)|Himmels]] und der [[Stern]]e“ bezogen. Nach umfangreichen Aufarbeitungen durch [[Harco Willems]]Harco Willems: ''The coffin of Heqata (Cairo JdE 36418): A case study of Egyptian funerary culture of the Early Middle Kingdom.'' Peeters, Leuven 1996, ISBN 90-6831-769-5, S. 262–270., [[Rolf Krauss]]Rolf Krauss: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5, S. 254–255. und [[Arno Egberts]]Arno Egberts: ''In quest of meaning: A study of the ancient Egyptian rites of consecrating the meret chests and driving the calves.'' Nederlands Institut voor het Nabije Oosten, Leiden 1995, ISBN 90-6258-208-7, S. 292–293. konnte der Bereich auf die Örtlichkeiten des [[Horizont]]s eingeschränkt werden. Mit den Ergebnissen der Untersuchungen des [[Papyrus]] [[pBM 47.218.50]] und der damit verbundenen Thematik der Mesqet-HeiligtümerJean-Claude Goyon: ''Confirmation du pouvoir royal au nouvel an. [Brooklyn Museum 47.218.50]. Planches'' (= ''Bibliothèque d'Étude.'' [BdE] Band 52). Institut Francais d'Archéologie Orientale (IFAO), Kairo 1972, S. 93–94. wurde schnell klar, dass sich die neueren Annahmen mit der Nennung im Brooklyn-Payprus decken, der explizit nur die Himmelsrichtungen Ost und West erwähnt. Schließlich belegte die Neubearbeitung des [[Nutbuch]]es die genaue Zuweisung in die Gebiete der Eingangstore der [[Duat]], die sich knapp über dem Horizont befinden. In den altägyptischen [[Ägyptisches Totenbuch|Totenbuchtexten]] wird die Region näher beschrieben: „Geht Re unter in der Qenqenet, kann er alles mögliche dort tun, und zwar in der Weise, wie gehandelt wird auf der [[Flammeninsel]],“ die in direktem Bezug zum gewundenen Duat-Wasserlauf [[Merencha]] steht und die Gebiete von [[Sechet-iaru]] umschließt: {{Zitat|[[Horus]], der [[Falken|Falke]] mit einer Länge von 1.000 [[Meh|Ellen]], geht auf und unter in der Qenqenet, sein Geburtsplatz, seine Wasserwege. Oh Qenqenet, ich bin in dich gekommen. Ich sah meinen Vater, ich erkannte meine Mutter. Als ich früh aufgestanden war, fing ich Fisch… Ich kenne den Namen dieses Gottes, dem Herrn der Heiligkeit. Wenn er erntet (in [[Sechet-iaru]]), werde ich zum Pflug und werde auch ernten.|Totenbuchspruch 110}} == Siehe auch == * [[Liste der ägyptischen Götter]] == Literatur == * [[Rolf Krauss]]: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * [[Bertha Porter]], [[Rosalind Moss|Rosalind L. B. Moss]]: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institut, Oxford 1994. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Einzelnachweise == [[Kategorie:Ort in der ägyptischen Mythologie]] [[Kategorie:Astronomie im Alten Ägypten]]

Specifically, it contrasts with the {{ill|Mesqet|de|Mesqet|12=draft}} and denotes the entrance area to the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|12=draft}}. == Background == [[File:Mock_mirage_of_the_setting_sun.jpg|alt=Qenqenet zone: Sunset over the horizon|thumb]] The Qenqenet is the place where {{ill|Ra (deity)|de|Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Ra|12=draft}}, {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|Baktiu|lt=Decan stars|de|Dekan-Stern|12=draft}} reside at the beginning of {{ill|Sunset|de|Sonnenuntergang|12=draft}}, just before they descend fully below the horizon. Mythologically, Qenqenet is therefore known as "the place before {{ill|Reincarnation|de|Wiedergeburt|12=draft}}." The location of Qenqenet was long debated among {{ill|Egyptologists|de|Ägyptologe|12=draft}}, as many texts also refer to it as "a region in the sky and the Duat." Qenqenet plays an important role in the {{ill|Pyramid Texts|de|Pyramidentexte|12=draft}}. Scholars such as {{ill|Kurt Sethe|de|Kurt Sethe|12=draft}},Kurt Sethe: ''Die altägyptischen Pyramidentexte. Nach den Papierabdrücken und Photographien des Berliner Museums. 1, Text, 1. Hälfte, Spruch 1-468 (Pyr. 1-905).'' Olms, Hildesheim 2001 (Nachdruck von 1908), ISBN 3-487-02593-0, S. 315. {{ill|Arielle Kozloff|de|Arielle Kozloff|12=draft}},Arielle Kozloff et al.: ''Egypt’s dazzling sun: Amenhotep III and his world.'' Cleveland Museum of Art in cooperation with Indiana University Press, Cleveland 1992, ISBN 0-940717-16-6, S. 336–337. and {{ill|Ronald Wells|de|Ronald Wells|12=draft}}Ronald Wells: ''The Mythology of Nut and the Birth of Ra.'' In: ''Studien zur altägyptischen Kultur.'' Band 19, 1992, S. 305–321. proposed that it could be the {{ill|Milky Way|de|Milchstraße|12=draft}}. However, further studies of other texts dismissed this hypothesis, as other evidence clearly referred to it as a "transitional region of the {{ill|Sky|de|Himmel (planetär)|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Stars|de|Stern|12=draft}}." Extensive studies by {{ill|Harco Willems|de|Harco Willems|12=draft}},Harco Willems: ''The coffin of Heqata (Cairo JdE 36418): A case study of Egyptian funerary culture of the Early Middle Kingdom.'' Peeters, Leuven 1996, ISBN 90-6831-769-5, S. 262–270. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=draft}},Rolf Krauss: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5, S. 254–255. and {{ill|Arno Egberts|de|Arno Egberts|12=draft}}Arno Egberts: ''In quest of meaning: A study of the ancient Egyptian rites of consecrating the meret chests and driving the calves.'' Nederlands Institut voor het Nabije Osten, Leiden 1995, ISBN 90-6258-208-7, S. 292–293. confined Qenqenet to the horizon's vicinity. Analysis of {{ill|Papyrus|de|Papyrus|12=draft}} {{ill|Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|de|pBM 47.218.50|12=draft}} concerning Mesqet sanctuariesJean-Claude Goyon: ''Confirmation du pouvoir royal au nouvel an. [Brooklyn Museum 47.218.50]. Planches'' (= ''Bibliothèque d'Étude.'' [BdE] Band 52). Institut Francais d'Archéologie Orientale (IFAO), Kairo 1972, S. 93–94. aligned with newer interpretations that identified Qenqenet explicitly with the eastern and western horizons. The {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch|12=draft}} clarified its assignment to the gateway regions of the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|12=draft}}, just above the horizon. In the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Book of the Dead|de|Ägyptisches Totenbuch|12=draft}}, Qenqenet is described in detail: "When Ra sets in Qenqenet, he can do anything there, just as one acts on the {{ill|Island of Flames|de|Flammeninsel|12=draft}}," which directly relates to the winding Duat watercourse {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|12=draft}} and the fields of {{ill|Aaru|de|Sechet-iaru|kk=Aaru|lt=Sekhet-iaru|12=draft}}." {{Quote|{{ill|Horus|de|Horus|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Falcon|de|Falken|12=draft}} 1,000 {{ill|Meh|de|Meh|12=draft}} long, rises and sets in Qenqenet, his birthplace and waterways. O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in {{ill|Aaru|lt=Sekhet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru|12=draft}}), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|12=draft}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=draft}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|de|Bertha Porter|12=draft}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind Moss|12=draft}}: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=draft}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Qenqenet]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Qenqenet]] [[de:Qenqenet]]

(Qen qenet)
''Qn qn.t'' }} '''Qenqenet''' refers to the celestial region just above the {{ill|Horizon|de|Horizont|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Astronomy in Ancient Egypt|de|Astronomie im Alten Ägypten|qq=draft}}. Specifically, it contrasts with the {{ill|Mesqet|de|Mesqet|qq=draft}} and denotes the entrance area to the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|qq=draft}}. == Background == [[File:Mock_mirage_of_the_setting_sun.jpg|alt=Qenqenet zone: Sunset over the horizon|thumb]] The Qenqenet is the place where {{ill|Ra (deity)|de|Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Ra|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|qq=draft}}, and the {{ill|Baktiu|lt=Decan stars|de|Dekan-Stern|qq=draft}} reside at the beginning of {{ill|Sunset|de|Sonnenuntergang|qq=draft}}, just before they descend fully below the horizon. Mythologically, Qenqenet is therefore known as "the place before {{ill|Reincarnation|de|Wiedergeburt|qq=draft}}." The location of Qenqenet was long debated among {{ill|Egyptologists|de|Ägyptologe|qq=draft}}, as many texts also refer to it as "a region in the sky and the Duat." Qenqenet plays an important role in the {{ill|Pyramid Texts|de|Pyramidentexte|qq=draft}}. Scholars such as {{ill|Kurt Sethe|de|Kurt Sethe|qq=draft}},Kurt Sethe: ''Die altägyptischen Pyramidentexte. Nach den Papierabdrücken und Photographien des Berliner Museums. 1, Text, 1. Hälfte, Spruch 1-468 (Pyr. 1-905).'' Olms, Hildesheim 2001 (Nachdruck von 1908), ISBN 3-487-02593-0, S. 315. {{ill|Arielle Kozloff|de|Arielle Kozloff|qq=draft}},Arielle Kozloff et al.: ''Egypt’s dazzling sun: Amenhotep III and his world.'' Cleveland Museum of Art in cooperation with Indiana University Press, Cleveland 1992, ISBN 0-940717-16-6, S. 336–337. and {{ill|Ronald Wells|de|Ronald Wells|qq=draft}}Ronald Wells: ''The Mythology of Nut and the Birth of Ra.'' In: ''Studien zur altägyptischen Kultur.'' Band 19, 1992, S. 305–321. proposed that it could be the {{ill|Milky Way|de|Milchstraße|qq=draft}}. However, further studies of other texts dismissed this hypothesis, as other evidence clearly referred to it as a "transitional region of the {{ill|Sky|de|Himmel (planetär)|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Stars|de|Stern|qq=draft}}." Extensive studies by {{ill|Harco Willems|de|Harco Willems|qq=draft}},Harco Willems: ''The coffin of Heqata (Cairo JdE 36418): A case study of Egyptian funerary culture of the Early Middle Kingdom.'' Peeters, Leuven 1996, ISBN 90-6831-769-5, S. 262–270. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft}},Rolf Krauss: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5, S. 254–255. and {{ill|Arno Egberts|de|Arno Egberts|qq=draft}}Arno Egberts: ''In quest of meaning: A study of the ancient Egyptian rites of consecrating the meret chests and driving the calves.'' Nederlands Institut voor het Nabije Osten, Leiden 1995, ISBN 90-6258-208-7, S. 292–293. confined Qenqenet to the horizon's vicinity. Analysis of {{ill|Papyrus|de|Papyrus|qq=draft}} {{ill|Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|de|pBM 47.218.50|qq=draft}} concerning Mesqet sanctuariesJean-Claude Goyon: ''Confirmation du pouvoir royal au nouvel an. [Brooklyn Museum 47.218.50]. Planches'' (= ''Bibliothèque d'Étude.'' [BdE] Band 52). Institut Francais d'Archéologie Orientale (IFAO), Kairo 1972, S. 93–94. aligned with newer interpretations that identified Qenqenet explicitly with the eastern and western horizons. The {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch|qq=draft}} clarified its assignment to the gateway regions of the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|qq=draft}}, just above the horizon. In the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Book of the Dead|de|Ägyptisches Totenbuch|qq=draft}}, Qenqenet is described in detail: "When Ra sets in Qenqenet, he can do anything there, just as one acts on the {{ill|Island of Flames|de|Flammeninsel|qq=draft}}," which directly relates to the winding Duat watercourse {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|qq=draft}} and the fields of {{ill|Aaru|de|Sechet-iaru|kk=Aaru|lt=Sekhet-iaru|qq=draft}}." {{Quote|{{ill|Horus|de|Horus|qq=draft}}, the {{ill|Falcon|de|Falken|qq=draft}} 1,000 {{ill|Meh|de|Meh|qq=draft}} long, rises and sets in Qenqenet, his birthplace and waterways. O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in {{ill|Aaru|lt=Sekhet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru|qq=draft}}), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|qq=draft}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|de|Bertha Porter|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind Moss|qq=draft}}: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Qenqenet]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:pages without German translation box]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] }} [[sn:Qenqenet]] [[de:Qenqenet]]

Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Qenqenet]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Qenqenet]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:11]] }} [[sn:Qenqenet]] [[de:Qenqenet]] {{translated page|de|Qenqenet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}}

Bot: cleanup/reformat
revision.text:
{{Infobox Hieroglyphen |NAME = N29:N35-N29:N35-X1 |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Qenqenet
(Qen qenet)
''Qn qn.t'' }} '''Qenqenet''' refers to the celestial region just above the {{ill|Horizon|de|Horizont|qq=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie|qq=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Astronomy in Ancient Egypt|de|Astronomie im Alten Ägypten|qq=draft|12=draft}}. Specifically, it contrasts with the {{ill|Mesqet|de|Mesqet|qq=draft|12=draft}} and denotes the entrance area to the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|qq=draft|12=draft}}. == Background == [[File:Mock_mirage_of_the_setting_sun.jpg|alt=Qenqenet zone: Sunset over the horizon|thumb]] The Qenqenet is the place where {{ill|Ra (deity)|de|Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Ra|qq=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|qq=draft|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|Baktiu|lt=Decan stars|de|Dekan-Stern|qq=draft|12=draft}} reside at the beginning of {{ill|Sunset|de|Sonnenuntergang|qq=draft|12=draft}}, just before they descend fully below the horizon. Mythologically, Qenqenet is therefore known as "the place before {{ill|Reincarnation|de|Wiedergeburt|qq=draft|12=draft}}." The location of Qenqenet was long debated among {{ill|Egyptologists|de|Ägyptologe|qq=draft|12=draft}}, as many texts also refer to it as "a region in the sky and the Duat." Qenqenet plays an important role in the {{ill|Pyramid Texts|de|Pyramidentexte|qq=draft|12=draft}}. Scholars such as {{ill|Kurt Sethe|de|Kurt Sethe|qq=draft|12=draft}},Kurt Sethe: ''Die altägyptischen Pyramidentexte. Nach den Papierabdrücken und Photographien des Berliner Museums. 1, Text, 1. Hälfte, Spruch 1-468 (Pyr. 1-905).'' Olms, Hildesheim 2001 (Nachdruck von 1908), ISBN 3-487-02593-0, S. 315. {{ill|Arielle Kozloff|de|Arielle Kozloff|qq=draft|12=draft}},Arielle Kozloff et al.: ''Egypt’s dazzling sun: Amenhotep III and his world.'' Cleveland Museum of Art in cooperation with Indiana University Press, Cleveland 1992, ISBN 0-940717-16-6, S. 336–337. and {{ill|Ronald Wells|de|Ronald Wells|qq=draft|12=draft}}Ronald Wells: ''The Mythology of Nut and the Birth of Ra.'' In: ''Studien zur altägyptischen Kultur.'' Band 19, 1992, S. 305–321. proposed that it could be the {{ill|Milky Way|de|Milchstraße|qq=draft|12=draft}}. However, further studies of other texts dismissed this hypothesis, as other evidence clearly referred to it as a "transitional region of the {{ill|Sky|de|Himmel (planetär)|qq=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Stars|de|Stern|qq=draft|12=draft}}." Extensive studies by {{ill|Harco Willems|de|Harco Willems|qq=draft|12=draft}},Harco Willems: ''The coffin of Heqata (Cairo JdE 36418): A case study of Egyptian funerary culture of the Early Middle Kingdom.'' Peeters, Leuven 1996, ISBN 90-6831-769-5, S. 262–270. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|12=draft}},Rolf Krauss: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5, S. 254–255. and {{ill|Arno Egberts|de|Arno Egberts|qq=draft|12=draft}}Arno Egberts: ''In quest of meaning: A study of the ancient Egyptian rites of consecrating the meret chests and driving the calves.'' Nederlands Institut voor het Nabije Osten, Leiden 1995, ISBN 90-6258-208-7, S. 292–293. confined Qenqenet to the horizon's vicinity. Analysis of {{ill|Papyrus|de|Papyrus|qq=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|de|pBM 47.218.50|qq=draft|12=draft}} concerning Mesqet sanctuariesJean-Claude Goyon: ''Confirmation du pouvoir royal au nouvel an. [Brooklyn Museum 47.218.50]. Planches'' (= ''Bibliothèque d'Étude.'' [BdE] Band 52). Institut Francais d'Archéologie Orientale (IFAO), Kairo 1972, S. 93–94. aligned with newer interpretations that identified Qenqenet explicitly with the eastern and western horizons. The {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch|qq=draft|12=draft}} clarified its assignment to the gateway regions of the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|qq=draft|12=draft}}, just above the horizon. In the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Book of the Dead|de|Ägyptisches Totenbuch|qq=draft|12=draft}}, Qenqenet is described in detail: "When Ra sets in Qenqenet, he can do anything there, just as one acts on the {{ill|Island of Flames|de|Flammeninsel|qq=draft|12=draft}}," which directly relates to the winding Duat watercourse {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|qq=draft|12=draft}} and the fields of {{ill|Aaru|de|Sechet-iaru|kk=Aaru|lt=Sekhet-iaru|qq=draft|12=draft}}." {{Quote|{{ill|Horus|de|Horus|qq=draft|12=draft}}, the {{ill|Falcon|de|Falken|qq=draft|12=draft}} 1,000 {{ill|Meh|de|Meh|qq=draft|12=draft}} long, rises and sets in Qenqenet, his birthplace and waterways. O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in {{ill|Aaru|lt=Sekhet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru|qq=draft|12=draft}}), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|qq=draft|12=draft}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|12=draft}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|de|Bertha Porter|qq=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind Moss|qq=draft|12=draft}}: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=draft}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == {{translated page|de|Qenqenet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Qenqenet]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Qenqenet]] [[de:Qenqenet]]

[Brooklyn Museum 47.218.50]. Planches'' (= ''Bibliothèque d'Étude.'' [BdE] Band 52). Institut Francais d'Archéologie Orientale (IFAO), Kairo 1972, S. 93–94. aligned with newer interpretations that identified Qenqenet explicitly with the eastern and western horizons. The {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch|qq=draft|qq=draft}} clarified its assignment to the gateway regions of the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, just above the horizon. In the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Book of the Dead|de|Ägyptisches Totenbuch|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Qenqenet is described in detail: "When Ra sets in Qenqenet, he can do anything there, just as one acts on the {{ill|Island of Flames|de|Flammeninsel|qq=draft|qq=draft}}," which directly relates to the winding Duat watercourse {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and the fields of {{ill|Aaru|de|Sechet-iaru|kk=Aaru|lt=Sekhet-iaru|qq=draft|qq=draft}}." {{Quote|{{ill|Horus|de|Horus|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the {{ill|Falcon|de|Falken|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 1,000 {{ill|Meh|de|Meh|qq=draft|qq=draft}} long, rises and sets in Qenqenet, his birthplace and waterways. O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in {{ill|Aaru|lt=Sekhet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|de|Bertha Porter|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind Moss|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == {{translated page|de|Qenqenet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Qenqenet]] [[de:Qenqenet]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = N29:N35-N29:N35-X1 |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Qenqenet
(Qen qenet)
''Qn qn.t'' }} '''Qenqenet''' refers to the celestial region just above the {{ill|Horizon|de|Horizont|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizon|qq=}} in {{ill|Egyptian mythology|de|Ägyptische Mythologie|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptian mythology|qq=}} and {{ill|Astronomy in Ancient Egypt|de|Astronomie im Alten Ägypten|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Astronomy in Ancient Egypt|qq=}}. Specifically, it contrasts with the {{ill|Mesqet|de|Mesqet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mesqet|qq=}} and denotes the entrance area to the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Duat|qq=}}. == Background == [[File:Mock_mirage_of_the_setting_sun.jpg|alt=Qenqenet zone: Sunset over the horizon|thumb]] The Qenqenet is the place where {{ill|Ra (deity)|de|Re (ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Ra|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ra (deity)|13=User:Immanuelle/Ra|qq=}}, {{ill|Horus|de|Horus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horus|qq=}}, and the {{ill|Baktiu|lt=Decan stars|de|Dekan-Stern|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} reside at the beginning of {{ill|Sunset|de|Sonnenuntergang|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sunset|qq=}}, just before they descend fully below the horizon. Mythologically, Qenqenet is therefore known as "the place before {{ill|Reincarnation|de|Wiedergeburt|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reincarnation|qq=}}." The location of Qenqenet was long debated among {{ill|Egyptologists|de|Ägyptologe|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptologists|qq=}}, as many texts also refer to it as "a region in the sky and the Duat." Qenqenet plays an important role in the {{ill|Pyramid Texts|de|Pyramidentexte|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Pyramid Texts|qq=}}. Scholars such as {{ill|Kurt Sethe|de|Kurt Sethe|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurt Sethe|qq=}},Kurt Sethe: ''Die altägyptischen Pyramidentexte. Nach den Papierabdrücken und Photographien des Berliner Museums. 1, Text, 1. Hälfte, Spruch 1-468 (Pyr. 1-905).'' Olms, Hildesheim 2001 (Nachdruck von 1908), ISBN 3-487-02593-0, S. 315. {{ill|Arielle Kozloff|de|Arielle Kozloff|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arielle Kozloff|qq=}},Arielle Kozloff et al.: ''Egypt’s dazzling sun: Amenhotep III and his world.'' Cleveland Museum of Art in cooperation with Indiana University Press, Cleveland 1992, ISBN 0-940717-16-6, S. 336–337. and {{ill|Ronald Wells|de|Ronald Wells|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronald Wells|qq=}}Ronald Wells: ''The Mythology of Nut and the Birth of Ra.'' In: ''Studien zur altägyptischen Kultur.'' Band 19, 1992, S. 305–321. proposed that it could be the {{ill|Milky Way|de|Milchstraße|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Milky Way|qq=}}. However, further studies of other texts dismissed this hypothesis, as other evidence clearly referred to it as a "transitional region of the {{ill|Sky|de|Himmel (planetär)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky|qq=}} and the {{ill|Stars|de|Stern|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stars|qq=}}." Extensive studies by {{ill|Harco Willems|de|Harco Willems|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harco Willems|qq=}},Harco Willems: ''The coffin of Heqata (Cairo JdE 36418): A case study of Egyptian funerary culture of the Early Middle Kingdom.'' Peeters, Leuven 1996, ISBN 90-6831-769-5, S. 262–270. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}},Rolf Krauss: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5, S. 254–255. and {{ill|Arno Egberts|de|Arno Egberts|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arno Egberts|qq=}}Arno Egberts: ''In quest of meaning: A study of the ancient Egyptian rites of consecrating the meret chests and driving the calves.'' Nederlands Institut voor het Nabije Osten, Leiden 1995, ISBN 90-6258-208-7, S. 292–293. confined Qenqenet to the horizon's vicinity. Analysis of {{ill|Papyrus|de|Papyrus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus|qq=}} {{ill|Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|de|pBM 47.218.50|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|qq=}} concerning Mesqet sanctuariesJean-Claude Goyon: ''Confirmation du pouvoir royal au nouvel an. [Brooklyn Museum 47.218.50]. Planches'' (= ''Bibliothèque d'Étude.'' [BdE] Band 52). Institut Francais d'Archéologie Orientale (IFAO), Kairo 1972, S. 93–94. aligned with newer interpretations that identified Qenqenet explicitly with the eastern and western horizons. The {{ill|Book of Nut|de|Nutbuch|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut|qq=}} clarified its assignment to the gateway regions of the {{ill|Duat|de|Duat|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Duat|qq=}}, just above the horizon. In the {{ill|Ancient Egyptian Book of the Dead|de|Ägyptisches Totenbuch|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Egyptian Book of the Dead|qq=}}, Qenqenet is described in detail: "When Ra sets in Qenqenet, he can do anything there, just as one acts on the {{ill|Island of Flames|de|Flammeninsel|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Island of Flames|qq=}}," which directly relates to the winding Duat watercourse {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|qq=}} and the fields of {{ill|Aaru|de|Sechet-iaru|kk=Aaru|lt=Sekhet-iaru|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aaru|qq=}}." {{Quote|{{ill|Horus|de|Horus|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horus|qq=}}, the {{ill|Falcon|de|Falken|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Falcon|qq=}} 1,000 {{ill|Meh|de|Meh|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meh|qq=}} long, rises and sets in Qenqenet, his birthplace and waterways. O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in {{ill|Aaru|lt=Sekhet-iaru|de|Sechet-iaru|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aaru|qq=}}), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian deities|qq=}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * {{ill|Bertha Porter|de|Bertha Porter|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bertha Porter|qq=}}, {{ill|Rosalind Moss|de|Rosalind Moss|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rosalind Moss|qq=}}: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == {{translated page|de|Qenqenet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Qenqenet]] [[de:Qenqenet]]

In: ''Studien zur altägyptischen Kultur.'' Band 19, 1992, S. 305–321. proposed that it could be the [[:en:Milky Way|Milky Way]]. However, further studies of other texts dismissed this hypothesis, as other evidence clearly referred to it as a "transitional region of the {{ill|Sky|de|Himmel (planetär)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky|qq=}} and the [[:en:Star|Stars]]." Extensive studies by {{ill|Harco Willems|de|Harco Willems|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harco Willems|qq=}},Harco Willems: ''The coffin of Heqata (Cairo JdE 36418): A case study of Egyptian funerary culture of the Early Middle Kingdom.'' Peeters, Leuven 1996, ISBN 90-6831-769-5, S. 262–270. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}},Rolf Krauss: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5, S. 254–255. and {{ill|Arno Egberts|de|Arno Egberts|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arno Egberts|qq=}}Arno Egberts: ''In quest of meaning: A study of the ancient Egyptian rites of consecrating the meret chests and driving the calves.'' Nederlands Institut voor het Nabije Osten, Leiden 1995, ISBN 90-6258-208-7, S. 292–293. confined Qenqenet to the horizon's vicinity. Analysis of [[:en:Papyrus|Papyrus]] {{ill|Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|de|pBM 47.218.50|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|qq=}} concerning Mesqet sanctuariesJean-Claude Goyon: ''Confirmation du pouvoir royal au nouvel an. [Brooklyn Museum 47.218.50]. Planches'' (= ''Bibliothèque d'Étude.'' [BdE] Band 52). Institut Francais d'Archéologie Orientale (IFAO), Kairo 1972, S. 93–94. aligned with newer interpretations that identified Qenqenet explicitly with the eastern and western horizons. The [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]] clarified its assignment to the gateway regions of the [[:en:Duat|Duat]], just above the horizon. In the [[:en:Book of the Dead|Ancient Egyptian Book of the Dead]], Qenqenet is described in detail: "When Ra sets in Qenqenet, he can do anything there, just as one acts on the {{ill|Island of Flames|de|Flammeninsel|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Island of Flames|qq=}}," which directly relates to the winding Duat watercourse {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|qq=}} and the fields of [[:en:Aaru|Sekhet-iaru]]." {{Quote|[[:en:Horus|Horus]], the [[:en:Falcon|Falcon]] 1,000 {{ill|Meh|de|Meh|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meh|qq=}} long, rises and sets in Qenqenet, his birthplace and waterways. O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in [[:en:Aaru|Sechet-iaru]]), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian deities|qq=}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * [[:en:Bertha Porter|Bertha Porter]], [[:en:Rosalind Moss|Rosalind Moss]]: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == {{translated page|de|Qenqenet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Qenqenet]] [[de:Qenqenet]]

{{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = N29:N35-N29:N35-X1 |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Qenqenet
(Qen qenet)
''Qn qn.t'' }} '''Qenqenet''' refers to the celestial region just above the [[:en:Horizon|Horizon]] in [[:en:Egyptian mythology|Egyptian mythology]] and [[:en:Egyptian astronomy|Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]]. Specifically, it contrasts with the {{ill|Mesqet|de|Mesqet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mesqet|qq=}} and denotes the entrance area to the [[:en:Duat|Duat]]. == Background == [[File:Mock_mirage_of_the_setting_sun.jpg|alt=Qenqenet zone: Sunset over the horizon|thumb]] The Qenqenet is the place where [[:en:Ra|Ra]], [[:en:Horus|Horus]], and the {{ill|Baktiu|lt=Decan stars|de|Dekan-Stern|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} reside at the beginning of [[:en:Sunset|Sunset]], just before they descend fully below the horizon. Mythologically, Qenqenet is therefore known as "the place before {{ill|Reincarnation|de|Wiedergeburt|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reincarnation|qq=}}." The location of Qenqenet was long debated among {{ill|Egyptologists|de|Ägyptologe|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptologists|qq=}}, as many texts also refer to it as "a region in the sky and the Duat." Qenqenet plays an important role in the [[:en:Pyramid Texts|Pyramid Texts]]. Scholars such as [[:en:Kurt Sethe|Kurt Sethe]],Kurt Sethe: ''Die altägyptischen Pyramidentexte. Nach den Papierabdrücken und Photographien des Berliner Museums. 1, Text, 1. Hälfte, Spruch 1-468 (Pyr. 1-905).'' Olms, Hildesheim 2001 (Nachdruck von 1908), ISBN 3-487-02593-0, S. 315. {{ill|Arielle Kozloff|de|Arielle Kozloff|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arielle Kozloff|qq=}},Arielle Kozloff et al.: ''Egypt’s dazzling sun: Amenhotep III and his world.'' Cleveland Museum of Art in cooperation with Indiana University Press, Cleveland 1992, ISBN 0-940717-16-6, S. 336–337. and {{ill|Ronald Wells|de|Ronald Wells|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronald Wells|qq=}}Ronald Wells: ''The Mythology of Nut and the Birth of Ra.'' In: ''Studien zur altägyptischen Kultur.'' Band 19, 1992, S. 305–321. proposed that it could be the [[:en:Milky Way|Milky Way]]. However, further studies of other texts dismissed this hypothesis, as other evidence clearly referred to it as a "transitional region of the {{ill|Sky|de|Himmel (planetär)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky|qq=}} and the [[:en:Star|Stars]]." Extensive studies by {{ill|Harco Willems|de|Harco Willems|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harco Willems|qq=}},Harco Willems: ''The coffin of Heqata (Cairo JdE 36418): A case study of Egyptian funerary culture of the Early Middle Kingdom.'' Peeters, Leuven 1996, ISBN 90-6831-769-5, S. 262–270. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}},Rolf Krauss: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5, S. 254–255. and {{ill|Arno Egberts|de|Arno Egberts|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arno Egberts|qq=}}Arno Egberts: ''In quest of meaning: A study of the ancient Egyptian rites of consecrating the meret chests and driving the calves.'' Nederlands Institut voor het Nabije Osten, Leiden 1995, ISBN 90-6258-208-7, S. 292–293. confined Qenqenet to the horizon's vicinity. Analysis of [[:en:Papyrus|Papyrus]] {{ill|Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|de|pBM 47.218.50|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|qq=}} concerning Mesqet sanctuariesJean-Claude Goyon: ''Confirmation du pouvoir royal au nouvel an. [Brooklyn Museum 47.218.50]. Planches'' (= ''Bibliothèque d'Étude.'' [BdE] Band 52). Institut Francais d'Archéologie Orientale (IFAO), Kairo 1972, S. 93–94. aligned with newer interpretations that identified Qenqenet explicitly with the eastern and western horizons. The [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]] clarified its assignment to the gateway regions of the [[:en:Duat|Duat]], just above the horizon. In the [[:en:Book of the Dead|Ancient Egyptian Book of the Dead]], Qenqenet is described in detail: "When Ra sets in Qenqenet, he can do anything there, just as one acts on the {{ill|Island of Flames|de|Flammeninsel|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Island of Flames|qq=}}," which directly relates to the winding Duat watercourse {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|qq=}} and the fields of [[:en:Aaru|Sekhet-iaru]]." {{Quote|[[:en:Horus|Horus]], the [[:en:Falcon|Falcon]] 1,000 {{ill|Meh|de|Meh|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meh|qq=}} long, rises and sets in Qenqenet, his birthplace and waterways. O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in [[:en:Aaru|Sechet-iaru]]), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian deities|qq=}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * [[:en:Bertha Porter|Bertha Porter]], [[:en:Rosalind Moss|Rosalind Moss]]: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == {{translated page|de|Qenqenet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Qenqenet]] [[de:Qenqenet]] [[Category:Astronomie im Alten Ägypten]]

When he harvests (in [[:en:Aaru|Sechet-iaru]]), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian deities|qq=}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == {{translated page|de|Qenqenet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Qenqenet]] [[de:Qenqenet]] [[Category:Astronomie im Alten Ägypten]] [[Category:Ort in der ägyptischen Mythologie]] [[pt:Qenqenet]]
