revision.text:
{{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = N29:N35-N29:N35-X1 |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Qenqenet
(Qen qenet)
''Qn qn.t'' }} '''Qenqenet''' refers to the celestial region just above the [[:en:Horizon|Horizon]] in [[:en:Egyptian mythology|Egyptian mythology]] and [[:en:Egyptian astronomy|Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]]. Specifically, it contrasts with the {{ill|Mesqet|de|Mesqet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mesqet|qq=}} and denotes the entrance area to the [[:en:Duat|Duat]]. == Background == [[File:Mock_mirage_of_the_setting_sun.jpg|alt=Qenqenet zone: Sunset over the horizon|thumb]] The Qenqenet is the place where [[:en:Ra|Ra]], [[:en:Horus|Horus]], and the {{ill|Baktiu|lt=Decan stars|de|Dekan-Stern|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} reside at the beginning of [[:en:Sunset|Sunset]], just before they descend fully below the horizon. Mythologically, Qenqenet is therefore known as "the place before {{ill|Reincarnation|de|Wiedergeburt|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reincarnation|qq=}}." The location of Qenqenet was long debated among {{ill|Egyptologists|de|Ägyptologe|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptologists|qq=}}, as many texts also refer to it as "a region in the sky and the Duat." Qenqenet plays an important role in the [[:en:Pyramid Texts|Pyramid Texts]]. Scholars such as [[:en:Kurt Sethe|Kurt Sethe]],Kurt Sethe: ''Die altägyptischen Pyramidentexte. Nach den Papierabdrücken und Photographien des Berliner Museums. 1, Text, 1. Hälfte, Spruch 1-468 (Pyr. 1-905).'' Olms, Hildesheim 2001 (Nachdruck von 1908), ISBN 3-487-02593-0, S. 315. {{ill|Arielle Kozloff|de|Arielle Kozloff|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arielle Kozloff|qq=}},Arielle Kozloff et al.: ''Egypt’s dazzling sun: Amenhotep III and his world.'' Cleveland Museum of Art in cooperation with Indiana University Press, Cleveland 1992, ISBN 0-940717-16-6, S. 336–337. and {{ill|Ronald Wells|de|Ronald Wells|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronald Wells|qq=}}Ronald Wells: ''The Mythology of Nut and the Birth of Ra.'' In: ''Studien zur altägyptischen Kultur.'' Band 19, 1992, S. 305–321. proposed that it could be the [[:en:Milky Way|Milky Way]]. However, further studies of other texts dismissed this hypothesis, as other evidence clearly referred to it as a "transitional region of the {{ill|Sky|de|Himmel (planetär)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky|qq=}} and the [[:en:Star|Stars]]." Extensive studies by {{ill|Harco Willems|de|Harco Willems|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harco Willems|qq=}},Harco Willems: ''The coffin of Heqata (Cairo JdE 36418): A case study of Egyptian funerary culture of the Early Middle Kingdom.'' Peeters, Leuven 1996, ISBN 90-6831-769-5, S. 262–270. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}},Rolf Krauss: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5, S. 254–255. and {{ill|Arno Egberts|de|Arno Egberts|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arno Egberts|qq=}}Arno Egberts: ''In quest of meaning: A study of the ancient Egyptian rites of consecrating the meret chests and driving the calves.'' Nederlands Institut voor het Nabije Osten, Leiden 1995, ISBN 90-6258-208-7, S. 292–293. confined Qenqenet to the horizon's vicinity. Analysis of [[:en:Papyrus|Papyrus]] {{ill|Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|de|pBM 47.218.50|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|qq=}} concerning Mesqet sanctuariesJean-Claude Goyon: ''Confirmation du pouvoir royal au nouvel an. [Brooklyn Museum 47.218.50]. Planches'' (= ''Bibliothèque d'Étude.'' [BdE] Band 52). Institut Francais d'Archéologie Orientale (IFAO), Kairo 1972, S. 93–94. aligned with newer interpretations that identified Qenqenet explicitly with the eastern and western horizons. The [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]] clarified its assignment to the gateway regions of the [[:en:Duat|Duat]], just above the horizon. In the [[:en:Book of the Dead|Ancient Egyptian Book of the Dead]], Qenqenet is described in detail: "When Ra sets in Qenqenet, he can do anything there, just as one acts on the {{ill|Island of Flames|de|Flammeninsel|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Island of Flames|qq=}}," which directly relates to the winding Duat watercourse {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|qq=}} and the fields of [[:en:Aaru|Sekhet-iaru]]." {{Quote|[[:en:Horus|Horus]], the [[:en:Falcon|Falcon]] 1,000 {{ill|Meh|de|Meh|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meh|qq=}} long, rises and sets in Qenqenet, his birthplace and waterways. O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in [[:en:Aaru|Sechet-iaru]]), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian deities|qq=}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * [[:en:Bertha Porter|Bertha Porter]], [[:en:Rosalind Moss|Rosalind Moss]]: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == {{translated page|de|Qenqenet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:Astronomy in Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Qenqenet]] [[de:Qenqenet]] [[Category:Ort in der ägyptischen Mythologie]] [[pt:Qenqenet]]

Qenqenet plays an important role in the [[:en:Pyramid Texts|Pyramid Texts]]. Scholars such as [[:en:Kurt Sethe|Kurt Sethe]],Kurt Sethe: ''Die altägyptischen Pyramidentexte. Nach den Papierabdrücken und Photographien des Berliner Museums. 1, Text, 1. Hälfte, Spruch 1-468 (Pyr. 1-905).'' Olms, Hildesheim 2001 (Nachdruck von 1908), ISBN 3-487-02593-0, S. 315. {{ill|Arielle Kozloff|de|Arielle Kozloff|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arielle Kozloff|qq=}},Arielle Kozloff et al.: ''Egypt’s dazzling sun: Amenhotep III and his world.'' Cleveland Museum of Art in cooperation with Indiana University Press, Cleveland 1992, ISBN 0-940717-16-6, S. 336–337. and {{ill|Ronald Wells|de|Ronald Wells|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronald Wells|qq=}}Ronald Wells: ''The Mythology of Nut and the Birth of Ra.'' In: ''Studien zur altägyptischen Kultur.'' Band 19, 1992, S. 305–321. proposed that it could be the [[:en:Milky Way|Milky Way]]. However, further studies of other texts dismissed this hypothesis, as other evidence clearly referred to it as a "transitional region of the {{ill|Sky|de|Himmel (planetär)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky|qq=}} and the [[:en:Star|Stars]]." Extensive studies by {{ill|Harco Willems|de|Harco Willems|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harco Willems|qq=}},Harco Willems: ''The coffin of Heqata (Cairo JdE 36418): A case study of Egyptian funerary culture of the Early Middle Kingdom.'' Peeters, Leuven 1996, ISBN 90-6831-769-5, S. 262–270. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}},Rolf Krauss: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5, S. 254–255. and {{ill|Arno Egberts|de|Arno Egberts|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arno Egberts|qq=}}Arno Egberts: ''In quest of meaning: A study of the ancient Egyptian rites of consecrating the meret chests and driving the calves.'' Nederlands Institut voor het Nabije Osten, Leiden 1995, ISBN 90-6258-208-7, S. 292–293. confined Qenqenet to the horizon's vicinity. Analysis of [[:en:Papyrus|Papyrus]] {{ill|Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|de|pBM 47.218.50|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|qq=}} concerning Mesqet sanctuariesJean-Claude Goyon: ''Confirmation du pouvoir royal au nouvel an. [Brooklyn Museum 47.218.50]. Planches'' (= ''Bibliothèque d'Étude.'' [BdE] Band 52). Institut Francais d'Archéologie Orientale (IFAO), Kairo 1972, S. 93–94. aligned with newer interpretations that identified Qenqenet explicitly with the eastern and western horizons. The [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]] clarified its assignment to the gateway regions of the [[:en:Duat|Duat]], just above the horizon. In the [[:en:Book of the Dead|Ancient Egyptian Book of the Dead]], Qenqenet is described in detail: "When Ra sets in Qenqenet, he can do anything there, just as one acts on the {{ill|Island of Flames|de|Flammeninsel|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Island of Flames|qq=}}," which directly relates to the winding Duat watercourse {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|qq=}} and the fields of [[:en:Aaru|Sekhet-iaru]]." {{Quote|[[:en:Horus|Horus]], the [[:en:Falcon|Falcon]] 1,000 {{ill|Meh|de|Meh|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meh|qq=}} long, rises and sets in Qenqenet, his birthplace and waterways. O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in [[:en:Aaru|Sechet-iaru]]), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian deities|qq=}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * [[:en:Bertha Porter|Bertha Porter]], [[:en:Rosalind Moss|Rosalind Moss]]: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == {{translated page|de|Qenqenet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Qenqenet]] [[de:Qenqenet]] [[Category:Ort in der ägyptischen Mythologie]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian astronomy]] [[pt:Qenqenet]]

{{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = N29:N35-N29:N35-X1 |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Qenqenet
(Qen qenet)
''Qn qn.t'' }} '''Qenqenet''' refers to the celestial region just above the [[:en:Horizon|Horizon]] in [[:en:Egyptian mythology|Egyptian mythology]] and [[:en:Egyptian astronomy|Ancient Egyptian astronomy]]. Specifically, it contrasts with the {{ill|Mesqet|de|Mesqet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mesqet|qq=}} and denotes the entrance area to the [[:en:Duat|Duat]]. == Background == [[File:Mock_mirage_of_the_setting_sun.jpg|alt=Qenqenet zone: Sunset over the horizon|thumb]] The Qenqenet is the place where [[:en:Ra|Ra]], [[:en:Horus|Horus]], and the {{ill|Baktiu|lt=Decan stars|de|Dekan-Stern|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} reside at the beginning of [[:en:Sunset|Sunset]], just before they descend fully below the horizon. Mythologically, Qenqenet is therefore known as "the place before {{ill|Reincarnation|de|Wiedergeburt|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reincarnation|qq=}}." The location of Qenqenet was long debated among {{ill|Egyptologists|de|Ägyptologe|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptologists|qq=}}, as many texts also refer to it as "a region in the sky and the Duat." Qenqenet plays an important role in the [[:en:Pyramid Texts|Pyramid Texts]]. Scholars such as [[:en:Kurt Sethe|Kurt Sethe]],Kurt Sethe: ''Die altägyptischen Pyramidentexte. Nach den Papierabdrücken und Photographien des Berliner Museums. 1, Text, 1. Hälfte, Spruch 1-468 (Pyr. 1-905).'' Olms, Hildesheim 2001 (Nachdruck von 1908), ISBN 3-487-02593-0, S. 315. {{ill|Arielle Kozloff|de|Arielle Kozloff|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arielle Kozloff|qq=}},Arielle Kozloff et al.: ''Egypt’s dazzling sun: Amenhotep III and his world.'' Cleveland Museum of Art in cooperation with Indiana University Press, Cleveland 1992, ISBN 0-940717-16-6, S. 336–337. and {{ill|Ronald Wells|de|Ronald Wells|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronald Wells|qq=}}Ronald Wells: ''The Mythology of Nut and the Birth of Ra.'' In: ''Studien zur altägyptischen Kultur.'' Band 19, 1992, S. 305–321. proposed that it could be the [[:en:Milky Way|Milky Way]]. However, further studies of other texts dismissed this hypothesis, as other evidence clearly referred to it as a "transitional region of the {{ill|Sky|de|Himmel (planetär)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky|qq=}} and the [[:en:Star|Stars]]." Extensive studies by {{ill|Harco Willems|de|Harco Willems|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harco Willems|qq=}},Harco Willems: ''The coffin of Heqata (Cairo JdE 36418): A case study of Egyptian funerary culture of the Early Middle Kingdom.'' Peeters, Leuven 1996, ISBN 90-6831-769-5, S. 262–270. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}},Rolf Krauss: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5, S. 254–255. and {{ill|Arno Egberts|de|Arno Egberts|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arno Egberts|qq=}}Arno Egberts: ''In quest of meaning: A study of the ancient Egyptian rites of consecrating the meret chests and driving the calves.'' Nederlands Institut voor het Nabije Osten, Leiden 1995, ISBN 90-6258-208-7, S. 292–293. confined Qenqenet to the horizon's vicinity. Analysis of [[:en:Papyrus|Papyrus]] {{ill|Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|de|pBM 47.218.50|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|qq=}} concerning Mesqet sanctuariesJean-Claude Goyon: ''Confirmation du pouvoir royal au nouvel an. [Brooklyn Museum 47.218.50]. Planches'' (= ''Bibliothèque d'Étude.'' [BdE] Band 52). Institut Francais d'Archéologie Orientale (IFAO), Kairo 1972, S. 93–94. aligned with newer interpretations that identified Qenqenet explicitly with the eastern and western horizons. The [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]] clarified its assignment to the gateway regions of the [[:en:Duat|Duat]], just above the horizon. In the [[:en:Book of the Dead|Ancient Egyptian Book of the Dead]], Qenqenet is described in detail: "When Ra sets in Qenqenet, he can do anything there, just as one acts on the {{ill|Island of Flames|de|Flammeninsel|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Island of Flames|qq=}}," which directly relates to the winding Duat watercourse {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|qq=}} and the fields of [[:en:Aaru|Sekhet-iaru]]." {{Quote|[[:en:Horus|Horus]], the [[:en:Falcon|Falcon]] 1,000 {{ill|Meh|de|Meh|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meh|qq=}} long, rises and sets in Qenqenet, his birthplace and waterways. O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in [[:en:Aaru|Sechet-iaru]]), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian deities|qq=}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * [[:en:Bertha Porter|Bertha Porter]], [[:en:Rosalind Moss|Rosalind Moss]]: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == {{translated page|de|Qenqenet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Qenqenet]] [[de:Qenqenet]] [[Category:Place in Egyptian Mythology]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian astronomy]] [[pt:Qenqenet]]

Institut Francais d'Archéologie Orientale (IFAO), Kairo 1972, S. 93–94. aligned with newer interpretations that identified Qenqenet explicitly with the eastern and western horizons. The [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]] clarified its assignment to the gateway regions of the [[:en:Duat|Duat]], just above the horizon. In the [[:en:Book of the Dead|Ancient Egyptian Book of the Dead]], Qenqenet is described in detail: "When Ra sets in Qenqenet, he can do anything there, just as one acts on the {{ill|Island of Flames|de|Flammeninsel|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Island of Flames|qq=}}," which directly relates to the winding Duat watercourse {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|qq=}} and the fields of [[:en:Aaru|Sekhet-iaru]]." {{Quote|[[:en:Horus|Horus]], the [[:en:Falcon|Falcon]] 1,000 {{ill|Meh|de|Meh|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meh|qq=}} long, rises and sets in Qenqenet, his birthplace and waterways. O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in [[:en:Aaru|Sechet-iaru]]), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian deities|qq=}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * [[:en:Bertha Porter|Bertha Porter]], [[:en:Rosalind Moss|Rosalind Moss]]: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == {{translated page|de|Qenqenet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Qenqenet]] [[de:Qenqenet]] [[Category:Places in Egyptian Mythology]] [[pt:Qenqenet]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = N29:N35-N29:N35-X1 |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Qenqenet
(Qen qenet)
''Qn qn.t'' }} '''Qenqenet''' refers to the celestial region just above the [[:en:Horizon|Horizon]] in [[:en:Egyptian mythology|Egyptian mythology]] and [[:en:Egyptian astronomy|Ancient Egyptian astronomy]]. Specifically, it contrasts with the {{ill|Mesqet|de|Mesqet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mesqet|qq=}} and denotes the entrance area to the [[:en:Duat|Duat]]. == Background == [[File:Mock_mirage_of_the_setting_sun.jpg|alt=Qenqenet zone: Sunset over the horizon|thumb]] The Qenqenet is the place where [[:en:Ra|Ra]], [[:en:Horus|Horus]], and the {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|lt=Decan stars|de|Dekan-Stern|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} reside at the beginning of [[:en:Sunset|Sunset]], just before they descend fully below the horizon. Mythologically, Qenqenet is therefore known as "the place before {{ill|Reincarnation|de|Wiedergeburt|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reincarnation|qq=}}." The location of Qenqenet was long debated among {{ill|Egyptologists|de|Ägyptologe|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptologists|qq=}}, as many texts also refer to it as "a region in the sky and the Duat." Qenqenet plays an important role in the [[:en:Pyramid Texts|Pyramid Texts]]. Scholars such as [[:en:Kurt Sethe|Kurt Sethe]],Kurt Sethe: ''Die altägyptischen Pyramidentexte. Nach den Papierabdrücken und Photographien des Berliner Museums. 1, Text, 1. Hälfte, Spruch 1-468 (Pyr. 1-905).'' Olms, Hildesheim 2001 (Nachdruck von 1908), ISBN 3-487-02593-0, S. 315. {{ill|Arielle Kozloff|de|Arielle Kozloff|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arielle Kozloff|qq=}},Arielle Kozloff et al.: ''Egypt’s dazzling sun: Amenhotep III and his world.'' Cleveland Museum of Art in cooperation with Indiana University Press, Cleveland 1992, ISBN 0-940717-16-6, S. 336–337. and {{ill|Ronald Wells|de|Ronald Wells|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronald Wells|qq=}}Ronald Wells: ''The Mythology of Nut and the Birth of Ra.'' In: ''Studien zur altägyptischen Kultur.'' Band 19, 1992, S. 305–321. proposed that it could be the [[:en:Milky Way|Milky Way]]. However, further studies of other texts dismissed this hypothesis, as other evidence clearly referred to it as a "transitional region of the {{ill|Sky|de|Himmel (planetär)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky|qq=}} and the [[:en:Star|Stars]]." Extensive studies by {{ill|Harco Willems|de|Harco Willems|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harco Willems|qq=}},Harco Willems: ''The coffin of Heqata (Cairo JdE 36418): A case study of Egyptian funerary culture of the Early Middle Kingdom.'' Peeters, Leuven 1996, ISBN 90-6831-769-5, S. 262–270. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}},Rolf Krauss: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5, S. 254–255. and {{ill|Arno Egberts|de|Arno Egberts|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arno Egberts|qq=}}Arno Egberts: ''In quest of meaning: A study of the ancient Egyptian rites of consecrating the meret chests and driving the calves.'' Nederlands Institut voor het Nabije Osten, Leiden 1995, ISBN 90-6258-208-7, S. 292–293. confined Qenqenet to the horizon's vicinity. Analysis of [[:en:Papyrus|Papyrus]] {{ill|Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|de|pBM 47.218.50|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|qq=}} concerning Mesqet sanctuariesJean-Claude Goyon: ''Confirmation du pouvoir royal au nouvel an. [Brooklyn Museum 47.218.50]. Planches'' (= ''Bibliothèque d'Étude.'' [BdE] Band 52). Institut Francais d'Archéologie Orientale (IFAO), Kairo 1972, S. 93–94. aligned with newer interpretations that identified Qenqenet explicitly with the eastern and western horizons. The [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]] clarified its assignment to the gateway regions of the [[:en:Duat|Duat]], just above the horizon. In the [[:en:Book of the Dead|Ancient Egyptian Book of the Dead]], Qenqenet is described in detail: "When Ra sets in Qenqenet, he can do anything there, just as one acts on the {{ill|Island of Flames|de|Flammeninsel|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Island of Flames|qq=}}," which directly relates to the winding Duat watercourse {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|qq=}} and the fields of [[:en:Aaru|Sekhet-iaru]]." {{Quote|[[:en:Horus|Horus]], the [[:en:Falcon|Falcon]] 1,000 {{ill|Meh|de|Meh|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meh|qq=}} long, rises and sets in Qenqenet, his birthplace and waterways. O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in [[:en:Aaru|Sechet-iaru]]), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian deities|qq=}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * [[:en:Bertha Porter|Bertha Porter]], [[:en:Rosalind Moss|Rosalind Moss]]: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == {{translated page|de|Qenqenet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Qenqenet]] [[de:Qenqenet]] [[Category:Places in Egyptian Mythology]] [[pt:Qenqenet]]

(Qen qenet)
''Qn qn.t'' }} '''Qenqenet''' refers to the celestial region just above the [[:en:Horizon|Horizon]] in [[:en:Egyptian mythology|Egyptian mythology]] and [[:en:Egyptian astronomy|Ancient Egyptian astronomy]]. Specifically, it contrasts with the {{ill|Mesqet|de|Mesqet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mesqet|qq=}} and denotes the entrance area to the [[:en:Duat|Duat]]. == Background == [[File:Mock_mirage_of_the_setting_sun.jpg|alt=Qenqenet zone: Sunset over the horizon|thumb]] The Qenqenet is the place where [[:en:Ra|Ra]], [[:en:Horus|Horus]], and the {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|lt=Decan stars|de|Dekan-Stern|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} reside at the beginning of [[:en:Sunset|Sunset]], just before they descend fully below the horizon. Mythologically, Qenqenet is therefore known as "the place before {{ill|Reincarnation|de|Wiedergeburt|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Reincarnation|qq=}}." The location of Qenqenet was long debated among {{ill|Egyptologists|de|Ägyptologe|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptologists|qq=}}, as many texts also refer to it as "a region in the sky and the Duat." Qenqenet plays an important role in the [[:en:Pyramid Texts|Pyramid Texts]]. Scholars such as [[:en:Kurt Sethe|Kurt Sethe]],Kurt Sethe: ''Die altägyptischen Pyramidentexte. Nach den Papierabdrücken und Photographien des Berliner Museums. 1, Text, 1. Hälfte, Spruch 1-468 (Pyr. 1-905).'' Olms, Hildesheim 2001 (Nachdruck von 1908), ISBN 3-487-02593-0, S. 315. {{ill|Arielle Kozloff|de|Arielle Kozloff|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arielle Kozloff|qq=}},Arielle Kozloff et al.: ''Egypt’s dazzling sun: Amenhotep III and his world.'' Cleveland Museum of Art in cooperation with Indiana University Press, Cleveland 1992, ISBN 0-940717-16-6, S. 336–337. and {{ill|Ronald Wells|de|Ronald Wells|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronald Wells|qq=}}Ronald Wells: ''The Mythology of Nut and the Birth of Ra.'' In: ''Studien zur altägyptischen Kultur.'' Band 19, 1992, S. 305–321. proposed that it could be the [[:en:Milky Way|Milky Way]]. However, further studies of other texts dismissed this hypothesis, as other evidence clearly referred to it as a "transitional region of the {{ill|Sky|de|Himmel (planetär)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky|qq=}} and the [[:en:Star|Stars]]." Extensive studies by {{ill|Harco Willems|de|Harco Willems|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harco Willems|qq=}},Harco Willems: ''The coffin of Heqata (Cairo JdE 36418): A case study of Egyptian funerary culture of the Early Middle Kingdom.'' Peeters, Leuven 1996, ISBN 90-6831-769-5, S. 262–270. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}},Rolf Krauss: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5, S. 254–255. and {{ill|Arno Egberts|de|Arno Egberts|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arno Egberts|qq=}}Arno Egberts: ''In quest of meaning: A study of the ancient Egyptian rites of consecrating the meret chests and driving the calves.'' Nederlands Institut voor het Nabije Osten, Leiden 1995, ISBN 90-6258-208-7, S. 292–293. confined Qenqenet to the horizon's vicinity. Analysis of [[:en:Papyrus|Papyrus]] {{ill|Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|de|pBM 47.218.50|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|qq=}} concerning Mesqet sanctuariesJean-Claude Goyon: ''Confirmation du pouvoir royal au nouvel an. [Brooklyn Museum 47.218.50]. Planches'' (= ''Bibliothèque d'Étude.'' [BdE] Band 52). Institut Francais d'Archéologie Orientale (IFAO), Kairo 1972, S. 93–94. aligned with newer interpretations that identified Qenqenet explicitly with the eastern and western horizons. The [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]] clarified its assignment to the gateway regions of the [[:en:Duat|Duat]], just above the horizon. In the [[:en:Book of the Dead|Ancient Egyptian Book of the Dead]], Qenqenet is described in detail: "When Ra sets in Qenqenet, he can do anything there, just as one acts on the {{ill|Island of Flames|de|Flammeninsel|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Island of Flames|qq=}}," which directly relates to the winding Duat watercourse {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|qq=}} and the fields of [[:en:Aaru|Sekhet-iaru]]." {{Quote|[[:en:Horus|Horus]], the [[:en:Falcon|Falcon]] 1,000 {{ill|Meh|de|Meh|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meh|qq=}} long, rises and sets in Qenqenet, his birthplace and waterways. O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in [[:en:Aaru|Sechet-iaru]]), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian deities|qq=}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * [[:en:Bertha Porter|Bertha Porter]], [[:en:Rosalind Moss|Rosalind Moss]]: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == {{translated page|de|Qenqenet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Qenqenet]] [[de:Qenqenet]] [[Category:Places in Egyptian Mythology]] [[pt:Qenqenet]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian astronomy]]

Analysis of [[:en:Papyrus|Papyrus]] {{ill|Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|de|pBM 47.218.50|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|qq=}} concerning Mesqet sanctuariesJean-Claude Goyon: ''Confirmation du pouvoir royal au nouvel an. [Brooklyn Museum 47.218.50]. Planches'' (= ''Bibliothèque d'Étude.'' [BdE] Band 52). Institut Francais d'Archéologie Orientale (IFAO), Kairo 1972, S. 93–94. aligned with newer interpretations that identified Qenqenet explicitly with the eastern and western horizons. The [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]] clarified its assignment to the gateway regions of the [[:en:Duat|Duat]], just above the horizon. In the [[:en:Book of the Dead|Ancient Egyptian Book of the Dead]], Qenqenet is described in detail: "When Ra sets in Qenqenet, he can do anything there, just as one acts on the {{ill|Island of Flames|de|Flammeninsel|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Island of Flames|qq=}}," which directly relates to the winding Duat watercourse {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|qq=}} and the fields of [[:en:Aaru|Sekhet-iaru]]." {{Quote|[[:en:Horus|Horus]], the [[:en:Falcon|Falcon]] 1,000 {{ill|Meh|de|Meh|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meh|qq=}} long, rises and sets in Qenqenet, his birthplace and waterways. O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in [[:en:Aaru|Sechet-iaru]]), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian deities|qq=}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * [[:en:Bertha Porter|Bertha Porter]], [[:en:Rosalind Moss|Rosalind Moss]]: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == {{translated page|de|Qenqenet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Qenqenet]] [[de:Qenqenet]] [[Category:Places in Egyptian Mythology]] [[pt:Qenqenet]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian astronomy]] [[Category:qq 4]]

{{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = N29:N35-N29:N35-X1 |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Qenqenet
(Qen qenet)
''Qn qn.t'' }} '''Qenqenet''' refers to the celestial region just above the [[:en:Horizon|Horizon]] in [[:en:Egyptian mythology|Egyptian mythology]] and [[:en:Egyptian astronomy|Ancient Egyptian astronomy]]. Specifically, it contrasts with the {{ill|Mesqet|de|Mesqet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mesqet|qq=}} and denotes the entrance area to the [[:en:Duat|Duat]]. == Background == [[File:Mock_mirage_of_the_setting_sun.jpg|alt=Qenqenet zone: Sunset over the horizon|thumb]] The Qenqenet is the place where [[:en:Ra|Ra]], [[:en:Horus|Horus]], and the {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|lt=Decan stars|de|Dekan-Stern|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} reside at the beginning of [[:en:Sunset|Sunset]], just before they descend fully below the horizon. Mythologically, Qenqenet is therefore known as "the place before [[:en:Reincarnation|Reincarnation]]." The location of Qenqenet was long debated among {{ill|Egyptologists|de|Ägyptologe|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptologists|qq=}}, as many texts also refer to it as "a region in the sky and the Duat." Qenqenet plays an important role in the [[:en:Pyramid Texts|Pyramid Texts]]. Scholars such as [[:en:Kurt Sethe|Kurt Sethe]],Kurt Sethe: ''Die altägyptischen Pyramidentexte. Nach den Papierabdrücken und Photographien des Berliner Museums. 1, Text, 1. Hälfte, Spruch 1-468 (Pyr. 1-905).'' Olms, Hildesheim 2001 (Nachdruck von 1908), ISBN 3-487-02593-0, S. 315. {{ill|Arielle Kozloff|de|Arielle Kozloff|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arielle Kozloff|qq=}},Arielle Kozloff et al.: ''Egypt’s dazzling sun: Amenhotep III and his world.'' Cleveland Museum of Art in cooperation with Indiana University Press, Cleveland 1992, ISBN 0-940717-16-6, S. 336–337. and {{ill|Ronald Wells|de|Ronald Wells|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronald Wells|qq=}}Ronald Wells: ''The Mythology of Nut and the Birth of Ra.'' In: ''Studien zur altägyptischen Kultur.'' Band 19, 1992, S. 305–321. proposed that it could be the [[:en:Milky Way|Milky Way]]. However, further studies of other texts dismissed this hypothesis, as other evidence clearly referred to it as a "transitional region of the {{ill|Sky|de|Himmel (planetär)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky|qq=}} and the [[:en:Star|Stars]]." Extensive studies by {{ill|Harco Willems|de|Harco Willems|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harco Willems|qq=}},Harco Willems: ''The coffin of Heqata (Cairo JdE 36418): A case study of Egyptian funerary culture of the Early Middle Kingdom.'' Peeters, Leuven 1996, ISBN 90-6831-769-5, S. 262–270. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}},Rolf Krauss: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5, S. 254–255. and {{ill|Arno Egberts|de|Arno Egberts|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arno Egberts|qq=}}Arno Egberts: ''In quest of meaning: A study of the ancient Egyptian rites of consecrating the meret chests and driving the calves.'' Nederlands Institut voor het Nabije Osten, Leiden 1995, ISBN 90-6258-208-7, S. 292–293. confined Qenqenet to the horizon's vicinity. Analysis of [[:en:Papyrus|Papyrus]] {{ill|Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|de|pBM 47.218.50|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|qq=}} concerning Mesqet sanctuariesJean-Claude Goyon: ''Confirmation du pouvoir royal au nouvel an. [Brooklyn Museum 47.218.50]. Planches'' (= ''Bibliothèque d'Étude.'' [BdE] Band 52). Institut Francais d'Archéologie Orientale (IFAO), Kairo 1972, S. 93–94. aligned with newer interpretations that identified Qenqenet explicitly with the eastern and western horizons. The [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]] clarified its assignment to the gateway regions of the [[:en:Duat|Duat]], just above the horizon. In the [[:en:Book of the Dead|Ancient Egyptian Book of the Dead]], Qenqenet is described in detail: "When Ra sets in Qenqenet, he can do anything there, just as one acts on the {{ill|Island of Flames|de|Flammeninsel|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Island of Flames|qq=}}," which directly relates to the winding Duat watercourse {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|qq=}} and the fields of [[:en:Aaru|Sekhet-iaru]]." {{Quote|[[:en:Horus|Horus]], the [[:en:Falcon|Falcon]] 1,000 {{ill|Meh|de|Meh|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meh|qq=}} long, rises and sets in Qenqenet, his birthplace and waterways. O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in [[:en:Aaru|Sechet-iaru]]), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian deities|qq=}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * [[:en:Bertha Porter|Bertha Porter]], [[:en:Rosalind Moss|Rosalind Moss]]: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == {{translated page|de|Qenqenet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Qenqenet]] [[de:Qenqenet]] [[Category:Places in Egyptian Mythology]] [[pt:Qenqenet]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian astronomy]] [[Category:qq 4]] [[Category:Missing wikidata]]

{{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = N29:N35-N29:N35-X1 |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Qenqenet
(Qen qenet)
''Qn qn.t'' }} '''Qenqenet''' refers to the celestial region just above the [[:en:Horizon|Horizon]] in [[:en:Egyptian mythology|Egyptian mythology]] and [[:en:Egyptian astronomy|Ancient Egyptian astronomy]]. Specifically, it contrasts with the {{ill|Mesqet|de|Mesqet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mesqet|qq=}} and denotes the entrance area to the [[:en:Duat|Duat]]. == Background == [[File:Mock_mirage_of_the_setting_sun.jpg|alt=Qenqenet zone: Sunset over the horizon|thumb]] The Qenqenet is the place where [[:en:Ra|Ra]], [[:en:Horus|Horus]], and the {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|lt=Decan stars|de|Dekan-Stern|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} reside at the beginning of [[:en:Sunset|Sunset]], just before they descend fully below the horizon. Mythologically, Qenqenet is therefore known as "the place before [[:en:Reincarnation|Reincarnation]]." The location of Qenqenet was long debated among {{ill|Egyptologists|de|Ägyptologe|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptologists|qq=}}, as many texts also refer to it as "a region in the sky and the Duat." Qenqenet plays an important role in the [[:en:Pyramid Texts|Pyramid Texts]]. Scholars such as [[:en:Kurt Sethe|Kurt Sethe]],Kurt Sethe: ''Die altägyptischen Pyramidentexte. Nach den Papierabdrücken und Photographien des Berliner Museums. 1, Text, 1. Hälfte, Spruch 1-468 (Pyr. 1-905).'' Olms, Hildesheim 2001 (Nachdruck von 1908), ISBN 3-487-02593-0, S. 315. {{ill|Arielle Kozloff|de|Arielle Kozloff|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arielle Kozloff|qq=}},Arielle Kozloff et al.: ''Egypt’s dazzling sun: Amenhotep III and his world.'' Cleveland Museum of Art in cooperation with Indiana University Press, Cleveland 1992, ISBN 0-940717-16-6, S. 336–337. and {{ill|Ronald Wells|de|Ronald Wells|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronald Wells|qq=}}Ronald Wells: ''The Mythology of Nut and the Birth of Ra.'' In: ''Studien zur altägyptischen Kultur.'' Band 19, 1992, S. 305–321. proposed that it could be the [[:en:Milky Way|Milky Way]]. However, further studies of other texts dismissed this hypothesis, as other evidence clearly referred to it as a "transitional region of the {{ill|Sky|de|Himmel (planetär)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky|qq=}} and the [[:en:Star|Stars]]." Extensive studies by {{ill|Harco Willems|de|Harco Willems|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harco Willems|qq=}},Harco Willems: ''The coffin of Heqata (Cairo JdE 36418): A case study of Egyptian funerary culture of the Early Middle Kingdom.'' Peeters, Leuven 1996, ISBN 90-6831-769-5, S. 262–270. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}},Rolf Krauss: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5, S. 254–255. and {{ill|Arno Egberts|de|Arno Egberts|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arno Egberts|qq=}}Arno Egberts: ''In quest of meaning: A study of the ancient Egyptian rites of consecrating the meret chests and driving the calves.'' Nederlands Institut voor het Nabije Osten, Leiden 1995, ISBN 90-6258-208-7, S. 292–293. confined Qenqenet to the horizon's vicinity. Analysis of [[:en:Papyrus|Papyrus]] {{ill|Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|de|pBM 47.218.50|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|qq=}} concerning Mesqet sanctuariesJean-Claude Goyon: ''Confirmation du pouvoir royal au nouvel an. [Brooklyn Museum 47.218.50]. Planches'' (= ''Bibliothèque d'Étude.'' [BdE] Band 52). Institut Francais d'Archéologie Orientale (IFAO), Kairo 1972, S. 93–94. aligned with newer interpretations that identified Qenqenet explicitly with the eastern and western horizons. The [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]] clarified its assignment to the gateway regions of the [[:en:Duat|Duat]], just above the horizon. In the [[:en:Book of the Dead|Ancient Egyptian Book of the Dead]], Qenqenet is described in detail: "When Ra sets in Qenqenet, he can do anything there, just as one acts on the {{ill|Island of Flames|de|Flammeninsel|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Island of Flames|qq=}}," which directly relates to the winding Duat watercourse {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|qq=}} and the fields of [[:en:Aaru|Sekhet-iaru]]." {{Quote|[[:en:Horus|Horus]], the [[:en:Falcon|Falcon]] 1,000 {{ill|Meh|de|Meh|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meh|qq=}} long, rises and sets in Qenqenet, his birthplace and waterways. O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in [[:en:Aaru|Sechet-iaru]]), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian deities|qq=}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * [[:en:Bertha Porter|Bertha Porter]], [[:en:Rosalind Moss|Rosalind Moss]]: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == [[Category:Translated page but missing wikidata]]{{translated page|de|Qenqenet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[de:Qenqenet]] [[Category:Places in Egyptian Mythology]] [[pt:Qenqenet]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian astronomy]] [[Category:Missing wikidata]]

However, further studies of other texts dismissed this hypothesis, as other evidence clearly referred to it as a "transitional region of the {{ill|Sky|de|Himmel (planetär)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky|qq=}} and the [[:en:Star|Stars]]." Extensive studies by {{ill|Harco Willems|de|Harco Willems|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harco Willems|qq=}},Harco Willems: ''The coffin of Heqata (Cairo JdE 36418): A case study of Egyptian funerary culture of the Early Middle Kingdom.'' Peeters, Leuven 1996, ISBN 90-6831-769-5, S. 262–270. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}},Rolf Krauss: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5, S. 254–255. and {{ill|Arno Egberts|de|Arno Egberts|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arno Egberts|qq=}}Arno Egberts: ''In quest of meaning: A study of the ancient Egyptian rites of consecrating the meret chests and driving the calves.'' Nederlands Institut voor het Nabije Osten, Leiden 1995, ISBN 90-6258-208-7, S. 292–293. confined Qenqenet to the horizon's vicinity. Analysis of [[:en:Papyrus|Papyrus]] {{ill|Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|de|pBM 47.218.50|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|qq=}} concerning Mesqet sanctuariesJean-Claude Goyon: ''Confirmation du pouvoir royal au nouvel an. [Brooklyn Museum 47.218.50]. Planches'' (= ''Bibliothèque d'Étude.'' [BdE] Band 52). Institut Francais d'Archéologie Orientale (IFAO), Kairo 1972, S. 93–94. aligned with newer interpretations that identified Qenqenet explicitly with the eastern and western horizons. The [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]] clarified its assignment to the gateway regions of the [[:en:Duat|Duat]], just above the horizon. In the [[:en:Book of the Dead|Ancient Egyptian Book of the Dead]], Qenqenet is described in detail: "When Ra sets in Qenqenet, he can do anything there, just as one acts on the {{ill|Island of Flames|de|Flammeninsel|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Island of Flames|qq=}}," which directly relates to the winding Duat watercourse {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|qq=}} and the fields of [[:en:Aaru|Sekhet-iaru]]." {{Quote|[[:en:Horus|Horus]], the [[:en:Falcon|Falcon]] 1,000 {{ill|Meh|de|Meh|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meh|qq=}} long, rises and sets in Qenqenet, his birthplace and waterways. O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in [[:en:Aaru|Sechet-iaru]]), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian deities|qq=}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * [[:en:Bertha Porter|Bertha Porter]], [[:en:Rosalind Moss|Rosalind Moss]]: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == {{translated page|de|Qenqenet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[de:Qenqenet]] [[Category:Places in Egyptian Mythology]] [[pt:Qenqenet]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian astronomy]] {{wikidata link|Q1300614}}

[[de:Qenqenet]][[pt:Qenqenet]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = N29:N35-N29:N35-X1 |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Qenqenet
(Qen qenet)
''Qn qn.t'' }} '''Qenqenet''' refers to the celestial region just above the [[:en:Horizon|Horizon]] in [[:en:Egyptian mythology|Egyptian mythology]] and [[:en:Egyptian astronomy|Ancient Egyptian astronomy]]. Specifically, it contrasts with the {{ill|Mesqet|de|Mesqet|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mesqet|qq=|WD=Q1922516}} and denotes the entrance area to the [[:en:Duat|Duat]]. == Background == [[File:Mock_mirage_of_the_setting_sun.jpg|alt=Qenqenet zone: Sunset over the horizon|thumb]] The Qenqenet is the place where [[:en:Ra|Ra]], [[:en:Horus|Horus]], and the {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|lt=Decan stars|de|Dekan-Stern|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} reside at the beginning of [[:en:Sunset|Sunset]], just before they descend fully below the horizon. Mythologically, Qenqenet is therefore known as "the place before [[:en:Reincarnation|Reincarnation]]." The location of Qenqenet was long debated among {{ill|Egyptologists|de|Ägyptologe|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptologists|qq=|WD=Q145903}}, as many texts also refer to it as "a region in the sky and the Duat." Qenqenet plays an important role in the [[:en:Pyramid Texts|Pyramid Texts]]. Scholars such as [[:en:Kurt Sethe|Kurt Sethe]],Kurt Sethe: ''Die altägyptischen Pyramidentexte. Nach den Papierabdrücken und Photographien des Berliner Museums. 1, Text, 1. Hälfte, Spruch 1-468 (Pyr. 1-905).'' Olms, Hildesheim 2001 (Nachdruck von 1908), ISBN 3-487-02593-0, S. 315. {{ill|Arielle Kozloff|de|Arielle Kozloff|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arielle Kozloff|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work}},Arielle Kozloff et al.: ''Egypt’s dazzling sun: Amenhotep III and his world.'' Cleveland Museum of Art in cooperation with Indiana University Press, Cleveland 1992, ISBN 0-940717-16-6, S. 336–337. and {{ill|Ronald Wells|de|Ronald Wells|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronald Wells|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work}}Ronald Wells: ''The Mythology of Nut and the Birth of Ra.'' In: ''Studien zur altägyptischen Kultur.'' Band 19, 1992, S. 305–321. proposed that it could be the [[:en:Milky Way|Milky Way]]. However, further studies of other texts dismissed this hypothesis, as other evidence clearly referred to it as a "transitional region of the {{ill|Sky|de|Himmel (planetär)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky|qq=|WD=Q527}} and the [[:en:Star|Stars]]." Extensive studies by {{ill|Harco Willems|de|Harco Willems|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harco Willems|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work}},Harco Willems: ''The coffin of Heqata (Cairo JdE 36418): A case study of Egyptian funerary culture of the Early Middle Kingdom.'' Peeters, Leuven 1996, ISBN 90-6831-769-5, S. 262–270. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=|WD=Q91239}},Rolf Krauss: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5, S. 254–255. and {{ill|Arno Egberts|de|Arno Egberts|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arno Egberts|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work}}Arno Egberts: ''In quest of meaning: A study of the ancient Egyptian rites of consecrating the meret chests and driving the calves.'' Nederlands Institut voor het Nabije Osten, Leiden 1995, ISBN 90-6258-208-7, S. 292–293. confined Qenqenet to the horizon's vicinity. Analysis of [[:en:Papyrus|Papyrus]] {{ill|Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|de|pBM 47.218.50|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work}} concerning Mesqet sanctuariesJean-Claude Goyon: ''Confirmation du pouvoir royal au nouvel an. [Brooklyn Museum 47.218.50]. Planches'' (= ''Bibliothèque d'Étude.'' [BdE] Band 52). Institut Francais d'Archéologie Orientale (IFAO), Kairo 1972, S. 93–94. aligned with newer interpretations that identified Qenqenet explicitly with the eastern and western horizons. The [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]] clarified its assignment to the gateway regions of the [[:en:Duat|Duat]], just above the horizon. In the [[:en:Book of the Dead|Ancient Egyptian Book of the Dead]], Qenqenet is described in detail: "When Ra sets in Qenqenet, he can do anything there, just as one acts on the {{ill|Island of Flames|de|Flammeninsel|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Island of Flames|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work}}," which directly relates to the winding Duat watercourse {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|qq=|WD=Q1571119}} and the fields of [[:en:Aaru|Sekhet-iaru]]." {{Quote|[[:en:Horus|Horus]], the [[:en:Falcon|Falcon]] 1,000 {{ill|Meh|de|Meh|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meh|qq=|WD=Q1777293}} long, rises and sets in Qenqenet, his birthplace and waterways. O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in [[:en:Aaru|Sechet-iaru]]), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian deities|qq=|WD=Q1076616}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=|WD=Q91239}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * [[:en:Bertha Porter|Bertha Porter]], [[:en:Rosalind Moss|Rosalind Moss]]: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == {{translated page|de|Qenqenet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[de:Qenqenet]] [[Category:Places in Egyptian Mythology]] [[pt:Qenqenet]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian astronomy]] {{wikidata link|Q1300614}}

In the [[:en:Book of the Dead|Ancient Egyptian Book of the Dead]], Qenqenet is described in detail: "When Ra sets in Qenqenet, he can do anything there, just as one acts on the {{ill|Island of Flames|de|Flammeninsel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Island of Flames|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work}}," which directly relates to the winding Duat watercourse {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|qq=|WD=Q1571119}} and the fields of [[:en:Aaru|Sekhet-iaru]]." {{Quote|[[:en:Horus|Horus]], the [[:en:Falcon|Falcon]] 1,000 {{ill|Meh|de|Meh|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meh|qq=|WD=Q1777293}} long, rises and sets in Qenqenet, his birthplace and waterways. O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in [[:en:Aaru|Sechet-iaru]]), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian deities|qq=|WD=Q1076616}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=|WD=Q91239}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * [[:en:Bertha Porter|Bertha Porter]], [[:en:Rosalind Moss|Rosalind Moss]]: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == {{translated page|de|Qenqenet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[de:Qenqenet]] [[Category:Places in Egyptian Mythology]] [[pt:Qenqenet]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian astronomy]] {{wikidata link|Q1300614}}

revision.comment: Bot: Add wikidata to ill templates
revision.text:
[[de:Qenqenet]][[pt:Qenqenet]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = N29:N35-N29:N35-X1 |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Qenqenet
(Qen qenet)
''Qn qn.t'' }} '''Qenqenet''' refers to the celestial region just above the [[:en:Horizon|Horizon]] in [[:en:Egyptian mythology|Egyptian mythology]] and [[:en:Egyptian astronomy|Ancient Egyptian astronomy]]. Specifically, it contrasts with the {{ill|Mesqet|de|Mesqet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mesqet|qq=|WD=Q1922516}} and denotes the entrance area to the [[:en:Duat|Duat]]. == Background == [[File:Mock_mirage_of_the_setting_sun.jpg|alt=Qenqenet zone: Sunset over the horizon|thumb]] The Qenqenet is the place where [[:en:Ra|Ra]], [[:en:Horus|Horus]], and the {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|lt=Decan stars|de|Dekan-Stern|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} reside at the beginning of [[:en:Sunset|Sunset]], just before they descend fully below the horizon. Mythologically, Qenqenet is therefore known as "the place before [[:en:Reincarnation|Reincarnation]]." The location of Qenqenet was long debated among {{ill|Egyptologists|de|Ägyptologe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptologists|qq=|WD=Q145903}}, as many texts also refer to it as "a region in the sky and the Duat." Qenqenet plays an important role in the [[:en:Pyramid Texts|Pyramid Texts]]. Scholars such as [[:en:Kurt Sethe|Kurt Sethe]],Kurt Sethe: ''Die altägyptischen Pyramidentexte. Nach den Papierabdrücken und Photographien des Berliner Museums. 1, Text, 1. Hälfte, Spruch 1-468 (Pyr. 1-905).'' Olms, Hildesheim 2001 (Nachdruck von 1908), ISBN 3-487-02593-0, S. 315. {{ill|Arielle Kozloff|de|Arielle Kozloff|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arielle Kozloff|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}},Arielle Kozloff et al.: ''Egypt’s dazzling sun: Amenhotep III and his world.'' Cleveland Museum of Art in cooperation with Indiana University Press, Cleveland 1992, ISBN 0-940717-16-6, S. 336–337. and {{ill|Ronald Wells|de|Ronald Wells|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronald Wells|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}Ronald Wells: ''The Mythology of Nut and the Birth of Ra.'' In: ''Studien zur altägyptischen Kultur.'' Band 19, 1992, S. 305–321. proposed that it could be the [[:en:Milky Way|Milky Way]]. However, further studies of other texts dismissed this hypothesis, as other evidence clearly referred to it as a "transitional region of the {{ill|Sky|de|Himmel (planetär)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky|qq=|WD=Q527}} and the [[:en:Star|Stars]]." Extensive studies by {{ill|Harco Willems|de|Harco Willems|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harco Willems|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}},Harco Willems: ''The coffin of Heqata (Cairo JdE 36418): A case study of Egyptian funerary culture of the Early Middle Kingdom.'' Peeters, Leuven 1996, ISBN 90-6831-769-5, S. 262–270. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=|WD=Q91239}},Rolf Krauss: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5, S. 254–255. and {{ill|Arno Egberts|de|Arno Egberts|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arno Egberts|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}Arno Egberts: ''In quest of meaning: A study of the ancient Egyptian rites of consecrating the meret chests and driving the calves.'' Nederlands Institut voor het Nabije Osten, Leiden 1995, ISBN 90-6258-208-7, S. 292–293. confined Qenqenet to the horizon's vicinity. Analysis of [[:en:Papyrus|Papyrus]] {{ill|Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|de|pBM 47.218.50|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} concerning Mesqet sanctuariesJean-Claude Goyon: ''Confirmation du pouvoir royal au nouvel an. [Brooklyn Museum 47.218.50]. Planches'' (= ''Bibliothèque d'Étude.'' [BdE] Band 52). Institut Francais d'Archéologie Orientale (IFAO), Kairo 1972, S. 93–94. aligned with newer interpretations that identified Qenqenet explicitly with the eastern and western horizons. The [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]] clarified its assignment to the gateway regions of the [[:en:Duat|Duat]], just above the horizon. In the [[:en:Book of the Dead|Ancient Egyptian Book of the Dead]], Qenqenet is described in detail: "When Ra sets in Qenqenet, he can do anything there, just as one acts on the {{ill|Island of Flames|de|Flammeninsel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Island of Flames|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}," which directly relates to the winding Duat watercourse {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|qq=|WD=Q1571119}} and the fields of [[:en:Aaru|Sekhet-iaru]]." {{Quote|[[:en:Horus|Horus]], the [[:en:Falcon|Falcon]] 1,000 {{ill|Meh|de|Meh|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meh|qq=|WD=Q1777293}} long, rises and sets in Qenqenet, his birthplace and waterways. O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in [[:en:Aaru|Sechet-iaru]]), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian deities|qq=|WD=Q1076616}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=|WD=Q91239}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * [[:en:Bertha Porter|Bertha Porter]], [[:en:Rosalind Moss|Rosalind Moss]]: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == {{translated page|de|Qenqenet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[de:Qenqenet]] [[Category:Places in Egyptian Mythology]] [[pt:Qenqenet]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian astronomy]] {{wikidata link|Q1300614}}

[[de:Qenqenet]][[pt:Qenqenet]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs |NAME = N29:N35-N29:N35-X1 |NAME-ERKLÄRUNG = Qenqenet
(Qen qenet)
''Qn qn.t'' }} '''Qenqenet''' refers to the celestial region just above the [[:en:Horizon|Horizon]] in [[:en:Egyptian mythology|Egyptian mythology]] and [[:en:Egyptian astronomy|Ancient Egyptian astronomy]]. Specifically, it contrasts with the {{ill|Mesqet|de|Mesqet|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mesqet|qq=|WD=Q1922516}} and denotes the entrance area to the [[:en:Duat|Duat]]. == Background == [[File:Mock_mirage_of_the_setting_sun.jpg|alt=Qenqenet zone: Sunset over the horizon|thumb]] The Qenqenet is the place where [[:en:Ra|Ra]], [[:en:Horus|Horus]], and the {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|lt=Decan stars|de|Dekan-Stern|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} reside at the beginning of [[:en:Sunset|Sunset]], just before they descend fully below the horizon. Mythologically, Qenqenet is therefore known as "the place before [[:en:Reincarnation|Reincarnation]]." The location of Qenqenet was long debated among {{ill|Egyptologists|de|Ägyptologe|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egyptologists|qq=|WD=Q145903}}, as many texts also refer to it as "a region in the sky and the Duat." Qenqenet plays an important role in the [[:en:Pyramid Texts|Pyramid Texts]]. Scholars such as [[:en:Kurt Sethe|Kurt Sethe]],Kurt Sethe: ''Die altägyptischen Pyramidentexte. Nach den Papierabdrücken und Photographien des Berliner Museums. 1, Text, 1. Hälfte, Spruch 1-468 (Pyr. 1-905).'' Olms, Hildesheim 2001 (Nachdruck von 1908), ISBN 3-487-02593-0, S. 315. {{ill|Arielle Kozloff|de|Arielle Kozloff|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arielle Kozloff|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}},Arielle Kozloff et al.: ''Egypt’s dazzling sun: Amenhotep III and his world.'' Cleveland Museum of Art in cooperation with Indiana University Press, Cleveland 1992, ISBN 0-940717-16-6, S. 336–337. and {{ill|Ronald Wells|de|Ronald Wells|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ronald Wells|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}Ronald Wells: ''The Mythology of Nut and the Birth of Ra.'' In: ''Studien zur altägyptischen Kultur.'' Band 19, 1992, S. 305–321. proposed that it could be the [[:en:Milky Way|Milky Way]]. However, further studies of other texts dismissed this hypothesis, as other evidence clearly referred to it as a "transitional region of the {{ill|Sky|de|Himmel (planetär)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sky|qq=|WD=Q527}} and the [[:en:Star|Stars]]." Extensive studies by {{ill|Harco Willems|de|Harco Willems|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Harco Willems|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}},Harco Willems: ''The coffin of Heqata (Cairo JdE 36418): A case study of Egyptian funerary culture of the Early Middle Kingdom.'' Peeters, Leuven 1996, ISBN 90-6831-769-5, S. 262–270. {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=|WD=Q91239}},Rolf Krauss: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5, S. 254–255. and {{ill|Arno Egberts|de|Arno Egberts|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arno Egberts|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}Arno Egberts: ''In quest of meaning: A study of the ancient Egyptian rites of consecrating the meret chests and driving the calves.'' Nederlands Institut voor het Nabije Osten, Leiden 1995, ISBN 90-6258-208-7, S. 292–293. confined Qenqenet to the horizon's vicinity. Analysis of [[:en:Papyrus|Papyrus]] {{ill|Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|de|pBM 47.218.50|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Papyrus Brooklyn 47.218.50|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}} concerning Mesqet sanctuariesJean-Claude Goyon: ''Confirmation du pouvoir royal au nouvel an. [Brooklyn Museum 47.218.50]. Planches'' (= ''Bibliothèque d'Étude.'' [BdE] Band 52). Institut Francais d'Archéologie Orientale (IFAO), Kairo 1972, S. 93–94. aligned with newer interpretations that identified Qenqenet explicitly with the eastern and western horizons. The [[:en:Book of Nut|Book of Nut]] clarified its assignment to the gateway regions of the [[:en:Duat|Duat]], just above the horizon. In the [[:en:Book of the Dead|Ancient Egyptian Book of the Dead]], Qenqenet is described in detail: "When Ra sets in Qenqenet, he can do anything there, just as one acts on the {{ill|Island of Flames|de|Flammeninsel|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Island of Flames|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}," which directly relates to the winding Duat watercourse {{ill|Merencha|de|Merencha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Merencha|qq=|WD=Q1571119}} and the fields of [[:en:Aaru|Sekhet-iaru]]." {{Quote|[[:en:Horus|Horus]], the [[:en:Falcon|Falcon]] 1,000 {{ill|Meh|de|Meh|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meh|qq=|WD=Q1777293}} long, rises and sets in Qenqenet, his birthplace and waterways. O Qenqenet, I have come into you. I saw my father, I recognized my mother. When I rose early, I caught fish… I know the name of this god, the Lord of Holiness. When he harvests (in [[:en:Aaru|Sechet-iaru]]), I will become the plow and will also harvest.|Book of the Dead, Spell 110}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Egyptian deities|de|Liste der ägyptischen Götter|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Egyptian deities|qq=|WD=Q1076616}} == References == * {{ill|Rolf Krauss|de|Rolf Krauss|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rolf Krauss|qq=|WD=Q91239}}: ''Astronomische Konzepte und Jenseitsvorstellungen in den Pyramidentexten.'' Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5. * [[:en:Bertha Porter|Bertha Porter]], [[:en:Rosalind Moss|Rosalind Moss]]: ''Topographical bibliography of ancient Egyptian hieroglyphic texts, reliefs, and paintings.'' Band 1: ''The Theban Necropolis; Private tombs.'' Griffith Institute, Oxford 1994. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch.'' The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies, Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 136–137. == Notes == {{translated page|de|Qenqenet|version=|comment=Imported full de history, revisions duplicated and version not here due to an error with my bot related to the German being under the same pagename as the english}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Locations in Egyptian mythology]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[de:Qenqenet]] [[Category:Places in Egyptian Mythology]] [[pt:Qenqenet]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian astronomy]] {{wikidata link|Q1300614}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 1997, ISBN 3-447-03979-5, S. 254–255. and {{ill|Arno Egberts|de|Arno Egberts|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Arno Egberts|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}Arno Egberts: ''In quest of meaning: A study of the ancient Egyptian rites of consecrating the meret chests and driving the calves.''
